Three Important Variables: Not Just Risk and Reward 
Early entries usually have smaller risk 
and greater reward (good RR), 
but lower probability of profit 
Stop 
Traders always have to consider 3 variables 
when planning to take any trade: 
Probability 
Reward 
Risk 
Can also t hink of them as just 2 variables: 
Probability 
Risk/Reward (RR) 
Later entries often have more certainty (higher probability), 
but pay for it with a worse RR 
6 
0 
because the stop is now far (more risk) and 
less remaining profit (smaller reward) 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 2 
@Albrooksall

Trading Choice: Good Risk/Reward (RR) or High Probability 
Stop 
For every trade, there is always one institution buying 
and another institution selling 
There always has to be a reason for both to trade 
if a trade is going to take place 
The 2 institutions are trading probability and RR 
One wants higher probability 
The other wants better RR 
0 + 
Buy or sell? Choose which variable is more important to you 
High probability: other side gets good reward/risk 
6 
0 
Small risk: other side gets good probability or reward 
Big reward: other side gets good probability or small risk 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 3 
@Albrooksall

Can make money with either 
high probability and weak RR 
or low probability and strong RR 
if manage the trades properly 
Stop 
When trader starts out, 
worked hard to save enough money to try trading 
Account is small 
Afraid of losing opportunity to see if can win big 
Generally take lowest risk trades 
Lose money because don't understand 
importance of probability and that 
low risk trades lose 60% or more of the time 
Lose money because of poor management 
6 
0 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 4 
@Albrooksall

Experienced Traders: Only Think About Math 
Stop 
They choose high probability or strong RR, 
depending on which gives them a better edge 
For example, if they have to leave soon, 
they might choose higher probability 
because these trades usually have a faster profit, 
even though is it usually smaller (scalp) 
Skilled traders don't worry too much 
about picking exact top or bottom 
Many wait for BO or pullback 
since probability 60% or more 
Comfortable with stop far away 
Bigger risk is cost of getting high probability 
6 
0 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 5 
@Albrooksall

One way to improve RR 
is by swing trading instead of scalping 
Initial risk 
Scalper takes profit here at lx risk 
Swing trader takes profit here 
Same initial risk as scalper 
Average profit is bigger 
More than offsets slightly lower probability of profit 
before stop is hit 
2nd way to improve Trader's Equation is by scaling in 
6 
0 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 6 
@Albrooksall

Scaling In: Increase Probability, but Pay for It with RR 
Scaling into trade increases probability 
Pay for it with increased risk and reduced reward 
Take profits at 1st entry 
Breakeven on 1st short 
Profit on 2nd short 
6 
0 
Sell below pullback bar 
Sell more (scale in) below Low 2 sell signal bar

High Probability: Means Scalping (Weak RR) 
0 
If someone wins 90% of time, he is scalping 
Cannot get good variable without giving on others 
If I get high probability, you get good RR ( usually small risk) 
This means that you lose a little and I make a little (scalp) 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 10 
+ 
@Albrooksall

RR Usually Exact: Probability Never Certain 
T 
You know risk and reward 
Sell close of 
2nd strong bear 
trend bar 
You set them when you place your bracket order 
Know how many ticks/pips they are from your entry price 
Risk = number of ticks/pips to protective stop 
Reward = number of ticks/pips to profit taking limit order 
Probability = most important, but never clear and often ignored 
Stop is above top of bear leg 
or top of 2 bar BO 
Reasonable initial 
profit target (reward) 
T 
Most traders want profit 
at least equal to initial risk

Slide 1 
@Albrooksall

• Used by Quants 
(quantitative analysts) 
to design systems 
■ Traders do not need Z-score (number 
of standard deviations from mean) 
• Measures reliability of 
back-testing 
• Can you trust your system? 
Z-Score = (N*(R-.S)-X)/((X*(X-N))/(N-l))A(l/2) 
N = Total number of trades in the series 
R = Total number of winning or 
losing streaks in the series 
X = 2*W*L 
W = Total number of winning trades 
in the series 
L = Total number of losing trades in the series 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 2 
@Albrooksall

Example: 
Scalp for lx risk on 2nd entry reversals 
Buy H2 Bull Flags and sell L2 Bear Flags

L

~+~ 
oss 
This system has a high Z score because it has 
high winning% (11 of 12 trades) and 
all winners about same size 
I 
Past results in this system are good predictor of future 
You can miss some trades and 
be confident that you will still make money

Probability: Most Important Variable, Not RR (Risk/Reward) 
Not take potentially great trades 
and instead wait for PBs 
If they do fin a 11 y s e 11, 
they exit trades too early, often for a loss 
Reason why so hard to make money is that 
probability scares and confuses beginners 
into making mistakes 
Wait too long until convinced bulls 
finally in control and then buy too late 
When PB comes, it looks like bull 
trend reversal so still do not sell

10% of bars are in strong BO or Tight Channel

Only trade with trend (here, sell) 
Cannot win until you are comfortable with constant uncertainty

to a profit target that is just as far?

lx risk

optimum profit target

As BO Grows: So Does Profit Target

New minimum target = 14 ticks 
from 2nd buy= size of initial risk

below bull BO,

new entry 
Target grows as BO grows 
BO is one or more strong bull trend bars 
BO is growing 
Bulls keep buying closes (''Buy The Close'' trend)

before hitting stop 14 ticks below new close 
That stop is still only 8 ticks below 1st buy entry 
and target is 20 ticks above of 1st buy 
Those bulls have 60% chance of making 20 ticks 
while risking only 8 ticks!

Slide 1 
@Albrooksall

Near Target: You Often Believe Probability Is 90°/o!! 
Stop 
Have limit order to take half off at prior high 
As soon as market reaches order, usually not filled 
Market usually has to go 
1 tick/pip above limit order to get filled 
Buy 50% PB in strong bull 
Looking to take profits on test of high 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 2 
@Albrooksall

Bad RR Can Be Good: Often Risk 10 Ticks/Pips to Make 1 
Stop 
You will hold for at least a few more seconds to 
get that one last tick/pip 
Stop still below bull leg or prior bull bar, so you 
are risking at least 10 ticks/pips to make 1 tick/pip 
You can only hold onto your trade if you 
believe that the probability is about 90% 
Otherwise, you would have losing Trader's Equation 
That would be irrational 
At the instant market touches 
profit taking limit order, 
it needs to go 1 tick/pip higher to fill the order 
Reward is now only 1 tick/pip, 
but risk might be 10 ticks/pips or more 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 3 
@Albrooksall

If Risk Greater Than Reward: Need 70°/o Winners 
Have to be right 70% of the time just to break even 
Most traders cannot scalp profitably day after day, year after year 
Should mostly swing trade (reward at least twice risk) 
Reasonable scalps that were profitable 
Looks like a good day! 
Most traders who took those winners 
would have taken these losers 
Even though 8 winners, this scalper lost money 
because he also took 12 losers! 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 4 
@Albrooksall

If Risk and Probability High: Skilled Traders Can Scalp 
Experienced traders can make money 
scalping during strong breakouts 
Many will scale in (see video on Scaling In) 
Take profits at lx actual risk instead 
of lx initial risk (see video on Actual Risk) 
T 
Stop, so initial risk 
Buy the close of strong bull BO 
Buy more lower 
Actual risk was here or higher 
Even though took profits at lx risk, 
Trader's Equation was positive 
because probability was 60%

Slide 6 
@Albrooksall

90% of time, probability is unclear 
Setups don't look strong 
Probability is 40 - 60% for moving X units in one direction 
before X units in opposite direction 
Applies to 90% of time = channel or TR 
(other 10% of bars= BO) 
Strong BO with follow-through is present 
during only 10% of bars 
If not obviously strong BO, 
assume channel or TR, since true for 90% of bars 
Assume probability for both bulls and bears is between 40 - 60% 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 7 
@Albrooksall

90o/o of Bars: In Channel or Trading Range 
Strong breakouts with follow-through are brief and infrequent 
For 90% of bars on every chart, 
traders swim in a sea of constant uncertainty 
Probability is 40 - 60% (mostly 40 - 50%) 
Don't need to know exactly 
■.6 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 8 
@Albrooksall

90°/o of Time: Both Bulls and Bears Can Make Money 
Simply always go for reward at least 2x risk 
Compensates for low probability 
Results in positive Trader's Equation 
Profitable strategy 
For 90% of bars, can structure both profitable long or short trade, 
even if win only 40 - 60% of trades 
Must manage well so that RR offsets low probability 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 9 
@Albrooksall

Bulls and bears can both have positive Trader's Equations! 
Probability of most swing trades is only 40 - 50% 
Opposite side therefore also has 40 - 50% chance 
Both the bulls and bears can lose more than 50% of time 
and still make money 
The key is money management 
Must use reasonable stop ( risk) 
Must try for reward that is at least 2x risk 
Overcomes probability of only 40% 
Keeps Trader's Equation positive (strategy is profitable)

If Everyone Knows PA: How Can It work? 
Charts look easy until you actually trade them! 
Trading is very difficult to do well! 
The margin for error is tiny 
Every little mistake works against you 
Best analysis right only about 60-70% of time 
Competing against best traders in world 
Almost Zero Sum Game (fees work against 
you, growing GDP is in your favor) 
They want your money 
Can watch Buffett for every minute for a year 
and still lose 
Huge difference between knowing how to play soccer, 
and playing it well enough to make a living

@Albrooksall

i 
In poker, good players decide who is the weakest player 
Easiest person to take money from is the ''mark'' 
If you don't know who the mark is, then you are the mark! 
In trading, if you don't know who is trapped, 
you will soon discover that it is you! 
If you take a trade and don't believe that 
exact opposite can happen at least 40% of time, 
you will be trapped in and out of positions!

■ What are swing trading and scalping? 
2 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 1 
@Albrooksall

Two Personality Types: Scalpers and Swing Traders 
Comes down to personality 
Scalper takes profit here at reward = risk 
or on reversal down 
Wants high probability, quick profit, and no PB 
Willing to accept small reward in return 
Scalper needs probability of 60 - 90% 
Reward is usually same as risk 
Scalper and 
swing trader 
both buy here 
Swing trader takes profit here 
Wants great risk/reward 
Willing to accept low probability in return 
Will accept pullbacks, expecting 2 or more legs 
Will hold for 10 or more bars waiting for profit 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 2 
@Albrooksall

Scalpers: Willing to Trade Minor Reversals 
Scalper will often trade minor reversals 
Minor reversal is small 
and a scalper only needs a small profit 
Scalper sells below bear bar after 
parabolic Buy Climax 
-- Stop 
Exits at bottom 
of Buy Climax bar 
Expects 2 legs down, 
knowing still Always In Long and 
trend likely to resume up 
Scalper will trade against trend if he thinks 
minor reversal will be at least as big as a scalp, 
and probability enough to offset required risk 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 3 
@Albrooksall

Swing Trader: Does Not Have to Exit If Minor Reversal 
Swing trader can hold onto position 
when minor reversal 
Swing trader buys close of follow-through bar 
after strong bull breakout 
Expects 2nd Leg Up after 
1st reversal down 
Stop 
Can rely on stop 
Take final profit since likely major reversal 
Final Flag, late In trend 
Nested Wedge Top 
Minor reversal means likely pullback and 
trend resumption, or at least a TR 
If TR, he can usually avoid loss by scaling in 
Major reversal means likely trend reversal 
or at least a TR 
Better to look to trade in opposite direction 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 4 
@Albrooksall

Swing Trader: Can Exit If Minor Reversal, If Can Enter Again 
Other swing traders exit if minor reversal has 
reasonable chance of becoming major 
Exits below bear bar after 
parabolic Wedge Buy Climax 
at Measured Move up 
Stop 
Can rely on stop 
Take final profit 
Since still Always In Long, 
buys again above bull bar 
50% pullback and test of breakout

Swing Trade: Means Major Reversal or BO 
General guidelines for what a swing trade is: 
M inimum for Emini is 4 points 
Minimum for Forex is 40 pips 
Minimum goal for $100 stock is SO cents 
Often not clear if trade was big scalp or small swing 
Most traders should mostly or only look to swing 
Traders want trend, lasting at least half of bars on screen 
Minimum goal is 2 legs and 10 bars, but want 20 or more bars 
(About 2 hours on 5 min chart) 
Reward should be at least twice as big as risk 
n•□,

Stop 
All reasonable swing entries 
Al I have same stop 
Minimum reward is based on distance to stop

Slide 9 
@Albrooksall

Scalp means reward is less than twice risk 
Scalper prefers profit within 1 - 5 bars and no PB 
Beginners should not scalp 
Looks easy, but very difficult to do profitably 
All of these are reasonable scalps 
(any trade is reasonable swing) 
Only for expert who can 
use swing stop and scale in lower 
This increases probability, but also increases risk 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 10 
@Albrooksall

Every Good Setup: Is Good Swing Setup 
When the market is Always In Long, 
swing traders can buy at any time and for any reason 
Must use the correct stop below Major Higher Low 
(bottom of most recent strong leg up) 
Must hold for a reward that is at least 2x risk 
Allow pullbacks, but not trend reversal 
Expect to hold for 10 or more bars 
Stop 
Raise stop after every 
strong bull BO, 
especially to new high 
Most scalpers can only trade when 
probability is at least 60% and 
can buy without scaling in 
When probability is high, traders can scalp or swing, 
but most should only swing on all trades

{58 pips) so swing trade

then risk is 29 pips

If reward is about 60 pips or more (twice risk), 
the trade is swing trade 
If less than 60 pips, it is scalp

@Albrooksall

Beginners should look for swings rather than scalps 
When scalping, with trend is better 
Beginners should never scalp against even a weak trend 
Many skilled traders buy for scalps in addition to swings 
in bull trend 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 2 
@Albrooksall

Beginners Desperate for Small Risk: So Sell Bull Trend 
Beginners believe correction is overdue (regression to mean) 
and trend can't go much further 
So afraid of losing, only consider risk 
Many beginners will only scalp against trend (here, sell) 
because risk is less (stop is close) 
Want to enter early before stop is far 
Beginners ignore reality that small stop means low probability 
and trading against trend means small reward 
Each loss will be greater than each winner 
More losers than winners 
Bleed to death from thousand paper cuts 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com

@Albrooksall

Here, Small PB Bull Trend and gaps (not stairs) so strong bull 
Experts only trade with trend (buy) 
Small PB Bull Trend 
Gap between low of PB bar and 
prior high (BO point)

Minimum Scalp Objective: For 5 Minute Emini Chart 
Emini 5 min 
5/29 
07:00 
Need minimum profit target 
Scalping on 5 min chart 
Emini and bond futures is 4 - 8 ticks 
Forex markets is 10 - 20 pips 
Stocks, commodities is 10 - 20 ticks 
5 - 10% of average day's range 
Average bar often about 1 - 2 points tall 
Scalp for about 1 -2 points

Minimum Scalp Objective: Most Markets 
If unclear what minimum scalp size is, 
make sure it is at least as big as both: 
Height of average recent bars 
5 - 10% of average recent daily ranges 
. I 
Recent bars are about 20 ticks tall 
Average daily range has been about 100, 
so 10% is 10 ticks 
Minimum scalp is the bigger of the two, 
so 20 ticks 
Exceptions: 
Emini is 4 ticks 
NQ is 2 - 4 points and US is 4 - 8 ticks, 
depending on how big bars are

Minimum Scalp Objective: Is Like Wanting Minimum Wage 
EURUSD 5 min 
23:30 
6/17 
00:30 
0 1:00 
0 1.30 
02:00 
The minimum grade to graduate college is a D 
Would you be happy if your son proudly told you that 
his goal in life was to get a D, or to make the minimum wage? 
Successful traders who take 10 pip scalps do so 
only when it is appropriate 
I have never met a trader who is making a living 
only going for minimum scalps 
There are many reasons for this, and overhead is one 
The combination of spread, commissions, slippage, 
and mistakes become proportionally so big that 
a trader needs to win 90% of trades consistently for years 
BrooksTradingCourse.com

While I know traders who do win 90% of the time, 
they do not go only for minimum profits 
If a trader restricts himself to tal<ing only trades 
where a 10 pip scalp is reasonable, 
he theoretically could be profitable (Paul Rotter is!) 
However, it is virtually impossible 
to do successfully long-term 
I just talked about reward (profit target) and probability, 
but there are 3 variables in the Trader's Equation, 
the 3rd is risk

for minimum profits will be stuck wondering why

Forex Example: 6 Pip Scalp, 100,000 EURUSD 
EURUSD 5 min 
23:30 
6/17 
00:30 
Suppose risking 6 pips to make 6 pips, and trade 100,000 EURUSD 
If bid/ ask spread = 2 pips each way, then 
1.1295 
need 8 pip move (6 + 2) to make 6 pips 
Winner = 6 pips x about $10/pip = $60 
Loser = 8 pips x about $10/pip = -$80 
Most of the time, 
commission (bid-ask spread} 
is relatively small 
If profit target is small, then commission is relatively big

Slide 1 
@Albrooksall

Most traders will only use 4 types of orders: 
Stop orders 
Limit orders 
Bracket orders 
Market orders 
These choices are available 
in the software that your broker gives you 
to enter and exit trades 
Most traders should almost always enter 
with stop orders 
To enter or exit a trade with a sell stop order, 
you place the order below the current price 
If the market reaches that price, 
your order gets filled 
To enter or exit a trade with a buy stop order, 
you place the order above the current price 
As soon as you enter, 
always place protective stop 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 2 
@Albrooksall

Order Types: Most Important Is Protective Stop 
Most important order for all trades 
is protective stop 
Protects you from huge loss 
if the market goes against you 
Protects you from yourself! 
When in a losing trade, 
beginners resist admitting they are wrong 
They don't want to take a loss 
Afraid their account will disappear 
± --11 
Death of dream of being a successful trader 
A bear places his stop above the current price 
If the market touches the stop, 
his order is filled and his position is closed 
A bull places his stop below the current price 
If the market touches the stop, 
his order is filled and his position is closed 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 3 
@Albrooksall

Black Swans: Protect Yourself from the Unknowable 
New, unanticipated market events exist and 
current systems are not designed 
to prevent them 
Black swan events are real 
Just because you never saw a black swan 
does not mean they do not exist 
However, it does mean they are uncommon 
and therefore low probability events 
± 
Software and hardware developers do not know 
what is unknowable 
They cannot design software and hardware 
to cover all possibilities 
All broker software and exchanges 
get overwhelmed and fail every 10 years or so, 
even though your broker will deny it 
I have seen this several times in the past 30 years 
1987 Crash, down 25% in one day 
2010 Flash Crash, down 9% in 36 minutes 
Before my time, 1929 Black Friday, 
down 90% in 3 years 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com

Limit Order: To Take Profits or to Enter Trade 
To enter or exit a trade with a limit order, 
market usually {90% of time) has to go 
through the limit order price 
before order is filled 
Buy above bull bar 
PB from BO to new high 
± 
Scalper might instead place 
his limit order to sell at prior high, 
as long as profit wou Id be 
at least as big as minimum scalp 
Bear scalper will sell 
with limit order there as well, 
betting on failed BO 
Scalper might want to exit at minimum scalp 
Places limit order to sell at that price 
Once market goes above, 
he knows he will be filled at {rarely above) his price 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 10 
@Albrooksall

Limit Order: To Take Profits or to Enter Trade 
To enter or exit a trade with a limit order, 
market usually (90% of time) has to go 
through the limit order price 
before order is filled 
Scalper might want to exit at minimum scalp 
Places limit order to buy at that price 
Once market goes below, 
l 
he knows he will be filled at (rarely below) his price 
Scalper might instead place 
his limit order to buy at prior low, 
as long as profit would be 
at least as big as minimum scalp 
Bear scalper will sell with limit order at prior high, 
betting on failed BO and reversal down 
Bull scalper might buy at prior low, 
betting on failed BO and reversal up 
(not yet here)

Bracket Order: To Take Profits and to Minimize Loss 
Bracket order is a combination of two orders 
used to exit trade: 
Profit taking limit order 
Protective stop order 
Also called ''OCO'' 
(''Order Cancels Order'' or 
''One Cancels Other'') 
Most traders should have a bracket order 
attached to their entry order 
As soon as you place your order to enter, 
software creates, but does not place, 
bracket order to exit 
Trader decides how far both orders should be 
They do not have to be same distance from entry 
Some traders use 2 or more limit orders 
to scale out of profits as market goes their way 
As soon as entry order is filled, 
software then places the bracket order 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 13 
@Albrooksall

Bracket Order for Short: Take Profits and Minimize Loss 
Bear places stop to sell below prior bar 
He has attached bracket order 
As soon as his sell order is filled, 
his broker places a bracket order 
Here, was planning to swing trade /' 
He placed his profit taking limit order 
far below his entry 
As soon as stop order got filled, 
software then cancelled his limit order 
Q 
Here, market hit his protective stop 
above the bear trend high 
Exited with loss

Bracket Order for Long: Take Profits and Minimize Loss 
Bull places stop to buy above prior bar 
He has attached bracket order 
As soon as his sell order is filled, 
his broker places a bracket order 
1 
Here, he placed a protective stop 
Here, was planning to scalp 
He placed his profit taking limit order 
at prior high 
As soon as limit order got filled, 
software then cancelled his stop order 
to exit with a loss below the buy entry bar

@Albrooksall

Most traders should almost always 
enter with stop order 
and exit with stop or limit order 
Sometimes trader has to make quick decision 
to exit or enter 
Sometimes better to get in or out immediately 
and not wait for stop to be hit 
or for pullback to fill limit order 
All software allows for ''Market Order'' 
If you place a buy market order to enter or exit, 
order will be immediately filled at the offer 
If you place a sell market order to enter or exit, 
order will be immediately filled at the bid

Other Orders: Many Brokers Offer Many Other Order Types 
Stop limit and marketable limit orders 
Mostly used for protection against 
floor traders and 
unethical brokers and firms 
Most traders never use them 
anymore now that computers 
fill orders 
Much better to avoid all other order types 
No benefit on most trades 
When beneficial, rarely much benefit 
Will make more money by focusing on 
managing trades well than using 
other types of orders 
Forex charts are not based on actual trades 
Often based on the bid or the ask, 
and there may never have been a trade 
at that price 
This means that the chart can look like 
a stop or limit order should have been filled, 
but it was not 
I discuss this in the videos on Forex Basics 
in the Main and Forex courses 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 21 
@Albrooksall

Broker Not Held: Mistakes Are Always Your Fault 
If the market reaches a price 
that should have filled your order, 
but your order does not get filled, 
call your broker 
99% of the time, you made a mistake with 
your order, but did not realize it 
1% of the time, it is the broker's fault 
or the exchange's fault 
When you opened your account, 
you signed your contract with the broker 
You agreed to a ''Not Held'' clause 
Broker is not held responsible for any 
problems filling or not filling orders 
If you are unhappy with a fill or no fill, 
it is always your fault, even if it is not

Bad for Stop Orders: Means Good for Limit Orders 
If direction not clear, like dojis, big tails, t hen 
probability is low for buy and sell stops entries 
If bars are big, then risk is big 
Low probability, big risk means 
bad math for stop orders and 
good math for limit orders 
Think of stop and limit orders 
as opposite side of same trade 
t 
Big bar so big risk 
Big bull bar, but probable TR after strong leg down 
Low probability buy 
More likely sellers above so bears sell above with limit order

Stop Orders and Limit Orders: Are Equal and Opposite 
For every trade, one institution is buying 
and another is selling, and both are rational trades 
Bulls buy with stop, betting on successful BO 
Bears sell with limit order, 
betting on failed BO and reversal down 
t 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 8 
@Albrooksall

Stop Orders and Limit Orders: Are Equal and Opposite 
Bulls buy with limit order, 
betting on failed bear BO and reversal up 
90% of time, both have 
Bears sell with stop order, 
betting on bear BO 
t 
equally valid Trader's Equations 
10% of the time, there is strong BO, and 
BO traders have better math

Slide 9 
@Albrooksall

Stop Orders and Limit Orders: Are Equally Effective 
On average, the probability that the market 
is going up or down is about the same 
Winning sell limit order 
A limit order (or market order) wins 
about as often as a stop order 
Beginners should only use stop orders 
(or market and limit orders in the

stop is below bottom of bull leg so big risk 
Buying at top of TR is low probability 
Stop

Better to look to enter with limit orders, 
selling near top, buying near bottom 
t 
Big risk and low probability means 
more likely bulls buying with stops will lose money 
Bears selling w ith limit orders will make money

is low probability 
Stop

more likely bears selling with stops will lose money

Skilled Traders: Always Have Protective Stop in the Market 
Stop protects you against yourself 
Emotions can influence decision making 
Without stop working in the market and 
not just in your head, you will let losses grow 
and grow, and grow 
Stop means stop what you are doing 
It is absolute 
Get out and don't question it 
Wait for next signal 
Several reasonable choices for stop 
Most would get out here, 
below bottom of 
most recent bull BO 
Does not matter where you buy 
Management is the same and is based on what the 
market is doing NOW, not when you bought 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 2 
@Albrooksall

Price action stop 
Most common and best for beginners 
Based on price action (low of bar, leg, or TR) 
Below support like below signal bar or bottom of 
most recent strong bull leg that broke to a new high 
Wide stop 
Buy Higher Low in early bull trend 
Strong bull reversal bar 
Most traders only think of stops as 
either wide or tight 
~ Many also use other terms 
Trailing stop 
After strong BO to new high, 
Raise stop to below bottom of bull leg 
Money stop 
10 ticks/pips below low of signal bar 
or $100 below entry 
Either money stop or price action stop 
Based on Measured Move 
or on higher time frame

Most pros get out on stop below 
bottom of most recent strong leg 
(major higher low}

since high probability of 2nd Leg Down after 
close on low and below low of past 20 bars 
Many chances to buy bull trend 
Others use wide stop below bottom of bull channel 
or below low of entire leg up

Breakeven Stop: Rarely Logical Choice 
Breakeven stop means moving stop 
to around entry price 
after the market goes your way 
0 
If sell and move stop to entry price, 
get out with either small gain or loss 
Breakeven does not have to be exact 
If stop is hit, 
only lose brokerage cost and slippage 
t 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com

@Albrooksall

Breakeven Stop: Rarely Logical Choice 
i. 
Breakeven stops are reasonable when confused or upset 
Good to exit if no longer objective 
0 
Breakeven stop is rarely best mathematical choice 
Entry price is not related to trade management 
t 
Always best to base stop on price action 
Breakeven stop makes sense when: 
Scalping 
2nd Breakout Test

Stop

Reversed up after getting 
to within 10% of profit target

Breakeven Stop: Often Not Exactly Breakeven 
If bears act quickly enough, 
can move stop down to 
above small buy setup 
0

Even if make small profit, still call it breakeven trade 
Breakeven means approximately breakeven 
and can be small profit or loss

Tight Stops: Cause Scalpers to Lose 
Very difficult to make a living as scalper 
Need to only take best scalps and trade huge size 
Risk (stop) usually has to be bigger than reward 
Requires unrealistic probability for most traders 
t 
+ 
Okay to take occasional scalps 
Focus on swing trading 
Can use reasonable stops and only 
need to be right 40% of time 
Scalps that many traders would take 
Al I were losers 
Stop entries in TTR are losers 
Expert scalpers increase probability 
by using swing stop and scaling in 
Has to match your personality 
Most good traders cannot do this profitably 
See videos on ''Scaling In''

Trader's Equation: Only Trade If Positive 
Only take a trade if: 
(Probability of success) x (Reward ) > (Probability of failure) x (Risk) 
• 
Stop 
Buy High 2 Bull Flag 
Deep PB, but held above bottom of bull BO 
Probability of profit is about 50% 
Risk is from entry price down to stop 
This is how much you are risking initially 
Different traders use different stops 
No agreement on Initial Risk 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 2 
@Albrooksall

Positive Trader's Equation: How Much Reward Needed? 
Can calculate smallest target needed for 
Trader's Equation to be positive 
This means the math supports the strategy 
and it is a good trade 
• 
Stop 
Reasonable minimum target 
for most trades is 2x risk 
Since probability not 60%, better to go for 
reward that is at least 2x risk

Perfect Stop: Clear After Trade Went Your Way 
After the trade goes your way, everyone can see 
where the best stop had to have been 
It is the smallest stop that allows you 
to stay in the trade without getting stopped out 
Impossible to know until after the trade 
goes strongly in your direction

Cannot hold trade forever 
Must have logical plan to take profits 
Many choices for initial stop (Initial Risk)

everyone agrees on 
Actual Risk and likely 
profit targets

to calculate where to take profits 
So should you!

Profit Targets in Bull: Are Resistance 
Traders look for reasons to take profits 
Math must be good 
Many take profits at 2x Actual Risk 
When probability is high, lx is okay 
Profit taking at lx and 2x often causes PB 
PB tells you that computers are taking profits 
Since profit targets lead to pullbacks, they are 
resistance in bull (sell off when bulls take profits) and 
support in bear (bounce when bears take profits) 
Some traders scalp in opposite direction at targets

Don't Make Conclusions From Big Loser: Sampling Error 
Not many bulls would buy 3rd push up in weak rally 
Expanding Triangle 
BO bar small and has tail 
Weak BO above possible TR 
t 
Initial Risk 
? 
This is not an average buy 
Does not accurately represent 
what a typical buy looks like 
Cannot expect it to represent entire 
collection of trades because most trades are 
much stronger and have smaller risk 
Most setups have higher probability 
and smaller risk 
This is an outlier

Big Wins and Big Losses: Sampling Error 
The extreme winners and losers are rare and 
tend to cancel one another out 
This is not a strong buy setup 
Most setups are higher probability 
and have smaller risk 
o 
Beware of sampling error 
If take 100 trades and go for lx risk when high probability and 
2x risk when low probability, odds of net profit are very high 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com

Small Sample: Must Represent All Trades, Not Rare Trades 
If only look at small sample of your 100 trades, 
make sure sample represents the average trade and 
not the extreme winners or losers

Initial Risk

who loses 20 ticks/SO pips on a 11 100 trades! 
But there will also be one lucky trader 
who makes 20 ticks/SO pips on all 100 trades!

Beginners Scale In: With Dollar Cost Averaging 
Dollar Cost Averaging 
Investment advisors often tell individual investors 
to not buy entire position at one time, because might 
end up buying the high of the next year or two 
For example, individual investor 
wants to buy 10,000 units of XYZ 
Plans to hold for 5 years 
Average entry price 
ri ?T • 
6 
-::L. 
o+ 
J. 
-l-D 
Buy 1,000 units on the 1st day 
of every month for 10 months 
Avoids the uncertainty of 
possibly buying too high

Trade Management: Comes after Entering 
Traders always take trades for a reason 
They have a goal (profit target) 
They protect themselves, 
if the goal is not achieved (stop) 
Buy PB from BO of TR 
Trade management comes after entering a trade 
It is following your plan, if the price action is what you expect 
It is changing your plan, if it is not 
Always be ready to exit, even with a loss, if premise no longer valid

OPM: Other People's Money Is Never True 
OPM is one of the dumbest concepts in trading 
Sell strong BO 
Strong Final Flag reversal 
Strong buy signal bar 
Beginner holds short, 
thinking that his open profit 
is other people's money 
Beginner moves stop to breakeven 
Doesn't worry about giving back profit, 
because it is OPM 
NO!! ONCE IN YOUR ACCOUNT, IT IS YOUR MONEY! 
Protect it 
This is a strong buy signal, so must exit shorts 
Never base your exit on your entry price 
Always manage trades based on current price action 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 9 
@Albrooksall

OPM Is Never True: Unless It Really Is! 
ONCE IN THEIR ACCOUNT, IT IS THEIR MONEY! 
Don't worry about it, because it is no longer yours 
You no longer have any control 
Buy PB from BO above 
Triangle Bottom 
Good signal bar 
Low probability, since 
Tight Bear Channel, 
so swing trade only 
3 
Stopped out 
with loss 
When you lose, don't look back with regret 
because your bad feelings will make you 
miss the next great trade 
Buy Final Flag 
Reversal

When Deciding How to Manage: Ignore Entry Price 
Traders should never manage trades based on where they entered 
Initial stop 
Does not matter where, 
or why you sold 
Some sold early 
Others sold later 
All that matters is: 
Where is stop? 
Where is target? 
Is premise still valid? 
This is a strong buy signal so must exit shorts, 
no matter where or why you shorted 
Always manage trades based on current price action

Structuring a Trade: Needs to Make Sense 
The key to making money is knowing how to structure a trade 
Decide: 
Whether to buy or sell 
How much to risk (where to put stop) 
What is profit goal (reward) 
T 
There is no one way to trade 
Goal of videos is to show many ways 
to structure mathematically 
reasonable trades

Default Targets: Reasonable Approach to Taking Profits 
Initial Risk 
2x Initial Risk 
3x Initial Risk 
Reasonable default plan for taking profits:

Take 25% more off at 3x Initial Risk 
Swing balance, trail the protective stop, exit before close 
Many ways to take profits 
Good to have default plan 
Can change to targets based on 
support and resistance at any time 
Look for reason to exit 
near end of session

Missed Trading a Huge Trend: Out of Fear? 
Emini 5 min 
Did you watch this and not buy? 
If so, probably out of fear that the stop was far away, 
which can cause a huge loss 
Beginners fear loss and miss great trades 
Understand what is happening and reduce the fear 
D 
Traders call this a ''FOMO'' trend 
(Fear Of Missing Out) 
Often creates Tight Channel, relentless trends 
because everyone is constantly buying small, 
fearful of a reversal, 
and afraid of missing out of a big move

Trend: Likely to Continue for at Least a Few More Bars? 
Emini 5 min 
After about 10 - 20 bars in a bull, 
are bulls acting as if bears are trapped? 
Is there a big bull BO? 
If so, bulls buying aggressively for swing up 
) 
Or, are bars small with tails on top and bear bodies? 
If so, bulls no longer confident 
Bulls now trying to scalp or exit breakeven 
Bears see weak bulls and are shorting

Wider stop has higher probability of profit, but loss is bigger

Some prefer high probability

@Albrooksall

Beginners want a perfect trade 
Want high probability setup that almost always makes money 
Want small risk and big profit so great risk/reward (RR) 
Perfect trades cannot exist 
No institution would ever take other side and have 
~ 
_.~ ·~+,' 
small chance of winning only a small profit 
while having a big risk 
Every trade has a tradeoff between 
probability and risk/reward (RR) 
If one side has a high probability of winning, 
other side has better RR

Choose what suits your personality better 
Some prefer high probability 
Others prefer small risk 
Tight stop means lose less per trade, but lose more often 
Wider stop has higher probability of profit, but loss is bigger 
As long as stop is reasonable, results are same after 100 trades 
Risking 2 ticks o·r 50 points is unreasonable 
Re·su Its in lasing strategy

t\ i 
Strong :bear BO (reversal) 
Clearly Always In Short 
Sell The Close 
Choose at least one entry

as soon as bar closes (Sell The Close Bears)

as soon as next bar begins, 
but sometimes do not get filled 
' t

traders exit quickly on reversal 
See video on Trading The End of The Day

Sell The Close: Sell Strong Bear Closes 
J. 
The reason why these big bear bars are 
closing on their lows is that 
institutions are selling just before the close 
Traders do the same 
0,+1 
:+t" 
''Sell The Close'' bears sell closes of strong or consecutive bear bars 
... ~ ~ 
as long as bars continue to close on their lows 
Most traders sell once and then swing trade 
Some experts keep selling more, increasing their position 
They are scaling into a winning trade

Once Clearly Always In Short: Look to Sell NOW! 
Strong bear BO (reversal) 
May not be a ''safe'', clear PB entry for long time 
Can never be more than 60% certain 
Don't be paralyzed by 40% chance of loss 
60% is as good as it gets 
If you are a trader, TRADE! 
If cannot decide which entry to sell, 
or if taking too long to enter, 
sell at market and use correct stop 
As you wait for smaller risk, you miss best part of trend 
Once you think Market Cycle changed to BO phase and 
Always In Short, sell at least small position

Every BO: Is at Least a Minor Climax 
Climax is an acceleration of a move up or down 
Extreme change in behavior 
Every BO (bar with big body) is a climax 
Most 80s (climaxes) are minor and are part of TRs or channels 
Most are followed by minor trends or minor reversals 
See videos on Trading in TRs 
and 
Trading Broad Channels

Whenever there is a big BO, 
especially after a trend lasts 20 or more bars, 
traders watch for evidence of either a 
Measuring Gap (MG) or an Exhaustion Gap (EG) 
Reversal up closed all gaps 
Bear BO became EG 
Climactic reversal, bears gave up 
Big bear BO late in trend 
Big bull BO late in trend 
Gap was start of new leg up 
Buy Climax became MG 
Gap between close of final bar of BO and: 
Low of bar before BO began 
Swing low 
Low of bear trend

Exhaustive Buy Climax: Beginners Should Exit Longs 
Whenever there is a big bull BO, 
especially after a trend lasts 20 or more bars, 
probability of reversal or end of bull is often 60% 
If beginner sees possible climactic end of bull trend, 
exit longs and do not look to buy 
Experts will also sell closes 
of strong bull trend bar (climax bar) 
Climax often has 
2nd climax before reversing 
• 
Beginner can sell reversal down, 
or sell close after 3 or more strong bear bars, 
or sell MTR 
Selling after reve·rsal begins is higher probability 
Always pay for this with worse risk/reward

Exhaustive Sell Climax: Beginners Should Exit Shorts 
Whenever there is a big bear BO, 
especially after a bear trend lasting 20 or more bars) 
probability of reversal or end of bear is often 60% 
If beginner sees possible climactic end of bear, 
exit shorts and do not look to sell 
Beginner can buy reversal up 
or buy close after 3 or more strong bull bars 
(not here), or MTR 
Experts will also buy close 
of strong bear trend bar (climax bar)

@Albrooksall

Only reason to distinguish a 
Tight Channel from a Broad Channel is 
because traders trade them differently 
When channel is broad, 
can make money buying or selling 
Since bull trend, usually higher 
probability and better risk/reward (RR) when buying 
When bull channel is tight like this one, 
very difficult to make money shorting 
Most PBs do not fall far enough

Tight Channels: How Do You Know If Tight? 
The more of these features present, the better it is to only buy: 
PBs last mostly 1 - 3 bars 
Size of PB is less than 2 - 3 times as big as smallest scalp (1 pt in Emini) 
Size of PB is less than 2 times the size of average bar 
Sell setups using stop entries are mostly losers, 
even if strong sell signal bar 
Average bar 
about 3 points tall 
Most PBs only 1/3rd to 1/2 
of prior bull leg 
Gaps between 
PB only 2 points 
PB and BO points 
If bear sells above and 
scales in with limit orders, 
losses offset gains

Lose Money If Sell Tight Bull Channel: Don't Fight Trend! 
Unlike Broad Channel, 
when Tight Channel, 
never trade against trend 
PBs are too small for bears 
Bears will lose money 
Tight Bull Channels always 
form Wedge ''Tops'' when bulls 
take partia I profits 
Bull trend ignores them 
and keeps going higher 
Weak bears sell below 
Weak bulls exit below

Sell Signal Bar in Tight Bull Channel: Buyers Below 
Beginner wants to trade, 
but wants small risk 
He knows that if he buys, stop is far and risk is big 
If he sells, stop is close and risk is small 
Looks for reasons to sell 
because stop is small 
Sees strong sell signal bar, 
Wedge, 
Failed BO above OT 
Ignores tightness of channel 
and therefore low probability 
Trader's Equation has 3 variables, 
and all are important 
Do NOT sell since 
probably buyers below

Tight Bear Channel: Bull Flag or Endless PB and Reversal? 
Reversal in bull trend is usually PB 
This means it is Bull Flag 
Often is Tight Bear Channel or Tight TR 
Look to buy, like on resumption up 
See videos on: 
Once it grows to 10 bars, 
traders begin to wonder if it is Endless PB 
and start of bear trend 
More likely if after Buy Climax, 
like Parabolic Wedge Top

and if 3 - 5 consecutive bear bars

lx Actual Risk

or with stop as market reverses down

evo Ive into a TR

□J. . ... 
Only reason to distinguish a 
Tight Channel from a Broad Channel is 
because traders trade them differently 
When channel is broad, 
can make money buying or selling 
Since bear trend, usually 
When bear channel is tight like this one, 
very difficult to make money buying 
Most PBs do not rally far enough 
higher probability and better risk/reward (RR) when selling

Tight Channels: How Do You Know If Tight? 
Average bar 
about 10 ticks tall 
Gaps between PB and BO points 
If bull buys below and 
scales in with limit orders, 
losses offset gains 
For stop entry buy, 
The more of these features present, the better it is to only sell: 
PBs fast mostly 1- 3 bars 
,

Tight Bear Channel: Bear Flag or Endless PB and Reversal? 
Reversal in bear trend is usually PB 
This means it is Bear Flag 
Often is Tight Bull Channel or Tight TR 
Look to sell, like on resumption down 
See videos on: 
Once it grows to 10 bars, 
traders begin to wonder if it is Endless PB 
and start of bull trend 
More likely if after Sell Climax, 
like Parabolic Wedge Bottom 
and if 3 - 5 consecutive bull bars 
Probability still favors Bear Flag 
unless strong bull BO and follow-through 
•

•

• 
Major Trend Reversals

Once Becomes TR: Stop Trading Like Trend 
After a trend lasts about 20 bars, 
look for signs that it might 
evo Ive into a TR 
Small TR, but 1st pause in strong 
bear usually becomes Bear Flag 
TR late in bear so stop trading like bear trend 
Probability of lower prices now no longer 60% 
50% chance of trend resumption 
50% chance of trend reversal

eventually become TRs

before evolving into TR

Broad Channels: How Do You Know If Broad? 
Emini 120 min 
The more of these features that are present, 
the more traders will both buy and sell 
Legs up and down lasting mostly 5 or more bars 
Size of average leg is at least 3 times bigger than 
smallest scalp (1 pt in Emini) 
Size of leg is at least 2 - 3 times 
the size of average bar 
PB 20 points 
Average bar 
about 8 points tall

Broad Channels: Why Not Common on 5 Min Chart? 
Emini 5 min 
Usually can not clearly form Broad Channel 
on 5 minute in one day, 
or on any chart unless there are at least 100 bars 
To believe it is bull channel and not TR, 
usually need at least 3 legs up and 3 legs down 
Possible HL in 3 day Broad Bull Channel 
It is also a TR and an early bear trend 
Not enough information for 
high probability trading

Broad Channels: Usually Not Enough Bars on 5 Min Chart 
Emini 5 min 
Since legs in Broad Channel often last 10 - 20 bars, 
usually not enough time to form channel 
in one day on 5 min chart 
Trade each segment as it is at the moment 
Change style of trading as behavior changes

Broad Channel on 5 Min chart: Usually Takes 2 or More Days 
Emini 5 min 
Broad Channels are much more common on 
60 min, daily, weekly, and monthly charts 
Broad Bull Channel often forms on 5 min chart 
over 2 - 3 days (here, 6 days) 
On 5 min chart, 6 day Broad Bull Channel 
can contain bear trends 
Sell HH MTR 
Sell LH MTR 
Buy HL MTR 
If trading 5 min chart, trade today 
based on patterns created today or 
that started yesterday

Major Low: Leads to Strong Rally and New High 
Strong new high so Major HL and still bull trend or TR 
Since possible TR, can get break below Major HL 
T·hen, either TR or bear trend 
Bulls buy reversal up 
2090.00 
2080.00 
After strong rally to new high, 
2010.00 
Strong rally to new high 
so Major Low 
If PB falls below bottom of rally, 
then no longer bull trend 
again now bull trend or TR 
Would be major change in Market Cycle 
from bull trend to TR or bear tre·nd 
HL has major importance

for bull trend to resume

@Albrooksall

Emini, 5 min, 9 days in Broad Bull Channel 
Broad Bull Channels form TRs 
TR contains topping patterns 
TR constantly tries to reverse down 
If PB falls below HL or TL, 
TR might be starting 
Reversal attempt breaks below old trend line, 
creating broader, flatter channel 
Eventually evolves into big TR (here, still in channel) 
All channels become broader and flatter 
with each new TR and as each TR grows

Bull channel and TR often have 2nd Leg Down 
that is strong enough to reverse to Always In Short 
60% chance of at least one more leg down 
Initially, only 40% chance of reversal back up, 
but reversal is often fast 
2

Always In Long again, 
with 60% chance of 
Measured Move 
(MM) up

that profit is too small for

Strong Bear BO Attempts: Reverse up Quickly 
Because bulls expect deep PBs and 
often wait to buy until in the bottom half, 
there can be a lack of buyers in the upper half 
This often creates a sell vacuum 
and a strong bear BO 
Bears think they won _________ ___ 
Once the bear BO reaches the buy zone {bottom 2/3rd5), 
bulls buy aggressively and overwhelm the bears, 
and create another HL

Broad Channels: How Do You Know If Broad? 
Emini 5 min 
The more of these features present, 
the more traders will both buy and sell 
Legs up and down lasting 
mostly 5 or more bars 
Size of average 'leg is at least 3 t imes 
bigger than smallest scalp (1 pt in Emini) 
Size of leg is at least 2 - 3 times 
the size of average bar

Today's 5 Min Chart Is Broad Channel: Wedge Bottom 
When Broad Bear Channel is within one day on 5 min chart, 
it is usually Wedge Bottom or a Trending TR day 
t 
Broad Bear Channel, but Wedge Bottom 
Traders expect reversal or TR, not more bear trend 
Not much time left for bear trend trade 
By the time traders are confident of Broad Bear Channel, 
not much time left for a swing trade down 
Buy Wedge Bottom 
Micro DB

Broad Channel on 5 Min Chart: Usually Takes 2 or More Days

can contain strong bull BOs and bull trends

Broad Bear Channel often forms on 5 min chart 
over 2 - 3 days (here, 9 days)

Emini 5 min, 9 days in Broad Bear Channel 
Broad Bear Channels form TRs 
TR contains bottom patterns 
TR constantly tries to reverse up 
Reversal attempt breaks above old trend line 
Creates broader, flatter channel 
If PB rallies above LH or trendline, 
TR might be starting 
All channels become broader and flatter 
with each new T·R and as each TR grows 
Eventually evolves into big TR (here, still in channel)

If Stop Not Hit and Premise Still Valid: Bulls Can Scale In

Some bulls scale in lower 
Increases probability of profit, 
but increases risk 
Trade small enough so risk not big 
Trading small reduces profit 
To get increased probability, 
must accept worse risk/reward

Bulls Take Profits: Need Minimum Scalp 
11/3 
Actual Risk is 4 ticks 
2x Actua I Risk is 8 ticks 
Minimum scalp size is 4 ticks in Emini 
Minimum reward is 1- 2x Actual Risk

Buy 2nd reversal up 
that profit is too small for chan,nel that is 10 points tall 
Try for bigger profit, like: 
Fixed number of points, like 4 - 5 points 
Around middle of channel {50% PB) 
Hold for swing up and exit on reversal down

Broad Bear Channel: Expect Deep PBs (Bear Rallies) 
Broad Bear Channel just a TR that is sloped down 
Bears expect deep PBs (rallies) 
Some bears often wait to sell 
until just below the last LH or 
even exactly at the LH (not here), 
forming a OT 
Need stop above 
most recent major LH 
Some bears sell close of big bull bar in 
top of channel or near MA ( not shown), 
betting bull BO will fail

Bear Channels: Evolve into TR or Bull Trend 
Channels do not last forever 
Once starts making HHs, then not bear trend 
Exit shorts and stop selling 
Once starts making Hls, then bull channel 
Only 25% chance of successful bear BO, 
then another bear channel 
and Measured Move down 
Bear channel is Bull Flag 
75% chance of evolving 
into bull trend or big TR

jo+ 
Spike and Channel 
bull trend

different probability 
of follow-through, 
depending on what came before

more likely bull leg in bull trend 
TR 
When BO is still below prior bars, 
more likely bull leg in TR

Confusion: Not as Good or Bad as It Appears 
Strong bear BO so possible bear trend 
1st rally will probably be sold for test back down 
Beginners believe huge bear trend 
Skilled traders think 60% chance of 
Measured Move (MM) down 
and 40% chance of reversal up 
Strong reversal up, but still LH 
1st reversal dow·n will probably 
be bought for test back up 
Big down, big up, so big confusion 
Disappointed bears and bulls 
Skilled traders now believe 
60% chance of more TR

but confident there is a top and bottom 
and that 80% of BOs will fail 
They sell near top and scale in higher and 
buy near the bottom and scale in lower

Early Sign of TR: Probabilities Not Behaving Well 
4 strong bull bars, weak sell signal bar, 
but big swing down 
If high probability setups do not seem to work often, 
and low probability setups seem to wo·rk too often, 
the market is probably in a TR 
Bull body, but got 2 legs down 
HL MTR, High 2, strong buy signal bar, 
but weak rally 
5 big bear bars, doji buy signal bar 
Low probability buy, but turned out to be good swing up

TR: Contained Between Support and Resistance 
Bulls and bears are balanced and alternate controt 
Bulls make money during bull legs 
Bears make money during bear legs 
I 
/ 
/ 
/ 
There is resistance at the top that 
prevents a rally from going too far up 
Ther-e is support below that 
keeps a selloff from falling too far

Early Signs of TR: If Many, Increased Chance of TR 
If traders see early signs of a TR, 
The more of signs of a TR that are present, the 
more traders will trade like the market is in a TR 
they will begin to trade like a TR rather than a trend 
Most legs, including the 1st leg, often have signs 
that make a TR more likely than a trend

80o/o Rule: Failed BO and Then Reversal Is Common 
Reversals and PBs after only 1- 3 bars 
Lack of consecutive big strong trend bars 
in bull swings and bear swings 
Bad follow-through after big trend bars 
. J 
i 
/
. 
/ 
Failed B0s above prior highs 
and below prior lows 
Traders betting on failures make money 
Markets have inertia 
They are resistant to change and tend to 
continue what they have been doing 
In TR, 80% of B0s up and down fail

Expect Gaps to Close: Not Lead to Trend 
Most gaps close

and high of prior bar 
Bear gaps a Isa closed

and closed the gap

from 14 bars ago 
Allowed bears who sold above prior bar to 
get out without a loss

rally more likely bull leg in TR than in bull trend

Bull Leg in TR: Instead of in Bull Trend 
Bears feel pattern and price are 
not strong enough to sell and 
do not want to sell until get better setup 
Bears are indifferent and waiting for better 
Going up because of lack of sellers, 
not because of enthusiastic buyers 
/ 
Bull leg in TR looks weak usually because 
bulls lack conviction 
Bulls are scalping out part or all, and 
not buying aggressively so 
no consecutive strong bull bars

All of the signs of a TR are caused by 
how traders are trading 
Traders are •Confident selloffs will not fall too far 
They buy any sign of 
bear strength near the bottom, 
or reversal up, betting the bear BO will fail 
/ 
V 
\ 
Traders are confident rallies will not go t o far 
They sell any sign of bull strength near the top, 
or reversal down, betting the bull BO will fail 
❖~ 
Since traders are confident 
that. moves will not go far, 
t 
they are quick to take profits (scalp)

Main Points: Buy in Bottom Half, Sell in Top Half 
■ 80% Rule 
■ Legs in TR, not in trend 
■ Buy in bottom half, sell in top half

Slide 14 
@Albrooksall

Bulls and Bears: Both Buy in Bottom Half, Sell in Top Half 
Bulls sell to take profits in the top half, 
where bears are shorting 
Bears buy to take profits in the bottom half, 
where bulls are buying 
With both bulls and bears buying low, selling high, 
and taking quick profits (scalping), 
the market continues sideways 
-
~ 
~ t 
When traders see evidence of a TR, 
they should do exactly 
what the institutions are doing 
Buy Low, Sell High, Scalp {BLSHS)

Slide 16 
@Albrooksall

LOM: Limit Order Market, Betting against 80s 
A stop entry order is a bet 
on a BO and a trend 
In TTR, scalping with stop orders 
loses so often that 
it is hard to make money with stop orders 
TRs contain many TTRs 
Limit Order Market (LOM): easier to make money 
selling above bars and buying below, 
betting B0s will fail 
Strong signal bars that are often big 
force traders to buy high or sell low 
Bad math: stop is far {risk is high), and TR, 
so stop orders are low probability

Most swings up and down have 2 legs 
Many legs subdivide into 2 legs 
Many legs subdivide into 3 legs (Wedges)

Look to sell reversal down from Wedge Top or 2 legs up

Trend Bars: Expect Bad Follow-Through 
If a bull BO needs 1 more bar 
to become clearly Always In Long, 
bears will sell the close, betting on 80% rule and 
betting against the next bar being a bull bar 
I 
I 
/ 
/ 
If a bear BO needs 1 more bar 
to become Always In Short, 
bulls will buy the close, betting on 80% rule and 
betting against the next bar being a bear bar

Scale In: To Make Profit or Reduce Loss 
Traders scale in to increase probability of profit 
or to reduce size or probability of loss 
Pay for this with increased risk so trade small 
-- Stop 
> 
I 
1/ 
When believe trade is bad, 
some scale in to reduce or prevent loss 
If bear sold at prior high, quickly 
became worried that leg up was too strong 
Possible bull trend 
instead· of 2nd Leg Trap in T,R 
Most never plan to let wide stop be hit 
Usually better to exit with loss 
when believe trade is bad 
Exit on close of follow-through bar

Stop 
Best choice for 
< 
scale in bears 
t 
Some hold short and rely on stop 
They scale in, hoping to reduce loss 
Whenever there is a surprise BO against 
a reasonable trade, a scale in trader has 
an 80% chance of getting out at least breakeven

Three choices for exit: 
1. At midpoint of both entries for 
breakeven on entire trade 
2. At 1st entry for breakeven on 1st sell and 
profit in 2nd sell (did not reach today) 
3. Exit at end of day

Wide Stop: Protect against Black Swan Catastrophes 
Purposes of wide stop are 
to give time to make decisions, 
and to protect against Black Swans 
Stop 
I 
/ 
V 
Trader always plans to exit 
before wide stop is hit 
Wide stop protects against Black Swans 
Low probability events that can create huge loss 
Internet or computer problems 
Exchange, broker and software problems 
Personal health and family emergencies

@Albrooksall

TR is horizontal channel 
Like all channels, can be broad or tight 
When broad, can enter with 
stop, market, or limit orders 
and can scalp or swing 
When tight, little profit potential 
Can only scalp and only enter with limit orders 
Never scalp with stop orders since most are losers 
Some use swing stops and scale in 
Most should wait

TR traders Buy Low, Sell High, and Scalp 
until strong BO with follow-through 
Bet on continued TR and failed BO 
If BO bar is big, 
closes near low, 
and has follow-through, 
traders believe TR has ended 
and bear trend has begun 
Bears sell bull BO, 
betting on reversal down 
Bulls give up and sell out of longs 
Bears sell, betting on bear trend 
And MM down 
Bulls buy bear BO, 
betting on reversal up 
Some scale in 
Only enter with limit orders if can make 
quick decisions and get out when wrong

Slide 1 
@Albrooksall

Traders think of every day as having 
an open, a middle, and an end 
On most days, it is easiest 
to make money quickly 
in the first and last hour or two 
This is the most common time for 
Breakouts (BO) and swing trades 
Volume is usually greater in the 1st and 
last hour or so 
~ 
' 
This indicates that 
traders are trading more intensely 
j~ t 
*tt 
Many traders trade less 
between these 2 times 
The result is that many traders 
think of the day as having 3 parts 
They realize that the transitions 
are usually not clear and that 
there are often only 2 segments to the day 
Sometimes 4 or more 
I 
11, 
I 
I ,, I 
• 
I I '• 'I 
l 
I I I 
I l 
I 
I 
I •r 
I 
~ 
However, many approach the day that way 
and look for 2 or more transitions every day 
Even if none appears, 
they are ready to change how they trade 
I 
I 
'• • I 
,. 
' 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 2 
@Albrooksall

A strong trend day trades in one direction 
all day long without any significant 
Pullback (PB) 
Only 10% of days are strong trend days 
Vil 
90% of the time, the price action changes 
Remember the Market Cycle 
There are only 3 things that markets can do 
BOs, bear and bull 
TR 
Channel 
Since 90% of days have 
at least 2 types of price action, 
traders are constantly looking for evidence 
of a transition from one to another 
When the price action changes, 
they change how they trade, 
I 
I 
which maximizes their profit potential

BrooksPriceAction.com

Although many traders think of the day 
as having 3 parts, that does not mean 
that there are 3 clear parts on the chart 
This is just how t raders think 
Sometimes smaller pieces are legs 
within larger channels or TRs 
'd 
The point is that most days 
are not strong trend days 
Most change character so 
look for possible transitions 
The 3 parts, when there are 3, 
do not each contain 1/3rd of the bars 
,, ' 1 
~f 
I 
I~ 
BOs, channels, and Trading Ranges (TR) 
need to be traded differently 
I 
Be aware when one changes into another 
Some days have only 1 type of pr.ice action 
I 
I I 
Others have 4 or more, and 
the transitions are often not clear

Start and End of Day: High Probability for Swing Trade 
In general, a swing trade in the Emini 
is one with.pt least a 4 point profit 
"·~ 
,...,,,.,  ... 
~lf.'!r(l)IOIII 
~Ji 
~ 
11,, 
I I 
~'°H' I 
There is a 90% chance of a swing trade 
beginning in the 1st 2 hours 
~ ., 
I 
There is an 80% chance of a swing trade j~, l 
beginning in the last 2 hours 
There is a 50% chance of a swing trade 
beginning in the middle of the day 
There are many swing trades 
in the middle that begin 
in the 1st 2 hours 
Day traders therefore try very hard 
to trade in the 1st and last 2 hours 
I 
111 
1111 
, .,, 
I 
Most trade all day long 
it· o·

usually has channels and TRs, 
traders often have to 
enter with limit orders and scalp more 
Trading is more work 
in the middle of the day

Most Important Rule: Be Prepared for Anything! 
The open often has surprising moves that seem 
too big or irrational to beginners 
Ex-perts do not care 
If the market is going up, they buy because it 
might go above all "reasonable" resistance 
If the market is going down, they sell because it 
might go below all 
11reasonable
11 support 
Trade what is there, 
Never have an opinion 
about what the market 
should do or not do 
even if you do not understand why 
it is doing what it is doing

is less important on the open

but it is not necessary, since the open has 
much more to do with today than with prior days

90% of days have a swing trade 
that starts in the first 90 minutes 
First one fails about half of time and 
is followed by opposite swing trade 
Bad follow-through 
after strong bull bars 
3 consecutive Buy Climaxes 
Wedge shape 
Deep PBs so broader channel 
Will it be the start of a trend day, 
or only a leg in a TR? 
If TR likely, then odds favor an 
opposite swing trade after 1 - 3 hours 
, 
3 consecutive strong bear trend bars 
Tight Bear Channel 
lij •• 
Attempt to reverse up from LL MTR, 
but bear channel too tight 
Got trend resumption down

to continue all day? 
Only 10% of days have 
no significant pause or reversal

that it is unsustainable? 
Parabolic 
Wedge

Consecutive Sell Climaxes

Are there strong trend bars, 
but weak follow-through bars? 
More common when trend is leg in TR 
TR has at least one leg down and one leg up, 
and either can come first 
If initial trend looks weak, be prepared for it 
to evolve into TR or opposite trend 
after 2 - 4 hours

Important function of middle 3rd is 
to test support or resistance 
The reaction from there 
determines direction of final 3rd 
When day is TR, and open of day is in middle 3rd, 
usually get test back to open in 2nd half of day

Tight TR is a Limit Order Market 
Do not trade 
Only skilled traders will consider 
trading limit orders at end of day, 
and they are very selective 
In final hour, most should only enter with 
stops or market orders in direction of trend 
Bars often big and reverse quickly 
Not enough time to decide on management 
rt~ ·~,~ 
I J..:.t 
Stop is about a 
Measured Move 
above or below TR 
Use correct stop 
Do not want to be stopped out if 
Limit Order Bu Is are buying near low 
Not enough ti me to decide 
Stop far away

and sells off down to it,

and market rallies to it (not here), 
bulls buy reversal up for 
possible close near high of day

on their highs, and with little overlap, there is 70% 
chance of at least a Measured Move (MM) up

follow-through bar~

Spike and Channel Bull Trend: Very Common Pattern 
90% of the time the breakout (BO) phase, 
is followed by a PB, and then a channel, 
or a Trading Range (TR) 
Low probabi lity Parabolic Wedge, 
since 1st Leg Up is on bear swing 
Only 10% of the time, 
the bull BO reverses, 
usually as a Parabolic Wedge Top 
Swing that begins on the open 
usually lasts at least 2 - 3 hours, 
but can be 30 min - 4 hours 
If th-en TR, can get late reversal down, 
instead of trend resumption up 
See videos on Trading Ranges

Limit Order Entries: Only for Skilled Traders 
Once the market is 
clearly controlled by the bears, 
bears will bet that 
a BO above a prior high will fail 
Weak bull BO 
Wedge Top 
Minor reversal, and 
TR more likely soon, 
than bull trend 
-- Stop 
Use stop above top of bear BO 
or above bear swing 
Some will scale in higher 
Any reasonable stop is good 
Results are about same after 10 trades 
Wide stops lose more, but less often 
Bears will sell at the high for swing or scalp

Consecutive Bottoms: Often Form High 4 Buy Setups 
When bottom fails, there are often then 
2 legs down and another bottom 
This is a High 
114" buy setup 
3 
1 
2 3 
High 4 buy setup is just a pair of consecutive bottoms 
where both have 2 - 3 legs down 
Sometimes 1st or 2nd bottom can 
be Wedge instead of High 2 
Here, both bottoms were Wedges, so actually High 6, 
but just a variation on Hig·h 4 
Principle is the same 
Bottom, BO, 2nd bottom 
Both bottoms each having at least 2 small legs 
+

Bear Stairs: Buy at Prior Low and Scale In Lower 
Trends tend to become weaker as they progress 
Once a stairs pattern develops, 
traders still bet it will continue, 
but expect start of transition into TR 
-
Bull scalpers will buy at the prior low 
If the BO falls for the size of a scalp, 
they will buy more 
Once the PB gets above the BO point, 
they get out breakeven on their 1st buy 
and with a profit on their lower buy

1 Min Chart: Most Should Avoid Since Many Mistakes 
Whenever a trader improves his risk/reward, 
he pays for it with slightly lower probability 
Makes 1 min chart less reliable than 5 min 
Bar can go from a bad setup to a great one, 
or visa versa, in a second 
1 min 
1972.00

1 Min Chart: Often Quick Move in Final Seconds 
Institutions often create a tail, or erase a tail, 
in the final second of the bar, 
to lower your probability (increase your mistakes) 
Surprises and confuses beginner 
Makes rushed, bad decisions 
Often quickly places order, 
then immediately decides he made mistake 
Often places order 1 - 2 ticks above or below 
where he intended 
More likely institutions will take your money 
1 min 
Most traders cannot consistently make money 
if chart has more than 20 bar/hour 
If use tick or volume chart, 
make sure to create one 
with no more than 20 bars per hour

1 Min Chart: Beginner Always Sees Reversals and Loses 
Looks easy, but beginners often 
buy too early above bad 1 min setups, 
and don't use wide enough stops 
Lose money buying in bull trend! 
Expert probably would not take that buy 
If he did, he would use wide stop 
He might scale in lower 
1 min 
Beginner takes low probability buy 
on 1 min chart, looking for 1 point (4 tick) scalp 
Stopped out for 6 tick loss

Scalping: Ticks Greater Than 700 - 1,000 (up or Down) 
NYSE has about 3,000 stocks 
If current trade of a stock is above last trade, adds +1 to Ticks 
If current trade of a stock is same as last trade, adds Oto Ticks 
If current trade of a stock is below last trade, adds -1 to Ticks 
Emini 1 min

2164 00

-300 
.goo 
Current Tick value is total 
for all stocks on NYSE 
If Ticks = 1,000, then 1,000 
more stocks went up 
than down on last trade 
Example, if 600 unchanged, 
and 2,200 went up, and 
1,200 went down, then 
0 + 2,200 - 1,200 = 1,000

Tick Divergence Buy Climax: Bears Sometimes Sell 
If Emini is at a confluence of resistance 
and there is a tick divergence at a new high 
where the 1st tick extreme was above 1,000, 
expert scalpers will sometimes sell the new high

Scalping: Most Traders Take Some Scalps 
I will show many examples of scalps that 
most traders can occasionally spot in time to take, 
and then manage well enough to be profitable 
Most of the time, you will spot them too late 
Don't worry about taking many 
Most of your money will come 
from swing trades, not scalps 
If you spot a scalp in time, and 
think you can manage it well, then take it

Strong Signal Bar: Bad When Context Is Bad 
Middle of TR and still below top of bear leg, 
so low probability of buyers above 
If bad buy for stop order bulls, then good sell 
Exit with limit order 
1 point below for 
1 point profit 
Sell close of big bull trend bar 
Big bar so big risk

BO: Need Follow-Through, Else It Is Trap and Will Reverse 
2 legs up after bear reversal 
Bulls need FT after BO above LH 
Bears bet on failed BO 
Exit with limit order 
1 point below entry for 
1 point profit 
Use limit order to sell close of bull BO bar 
or at prior LH

Sell The Close Trend: Usually Ends After 3 - 6 Bars 
Sell The Close bear trend has ended 
Bears now change goal 
Bars are big and stop is far 
Try to scale in 2 pts higher, not just 1 pt higher 
Try to scale in 4 pts higher, but did not reach order 
Exit at 1st entry 
Breakeven on 1st short 
2 pts profit on 2nd short 
Use limit order to sell more 2 pts higher

90°/o of Bars: Bulls and Bears Can Make Money 
Bull BO, but bulls need follow-through 
Strong enough BO, likely at least small 2nd Leg Up 
Bear trend usually evolves into TR, not bull trend 
Possible 2nd Leg Trap and failed bull BO in TR 
Wedge Bear Flag, LH, possible bear channel 
Bulls can buy PB 
Bears can sell close and scale in higher 
TR likely so bulls can buy and bears can sell, 
and both make money 
if use wide stops and scale in

Bear BO: Always Think of Measuring Gap 
3 consecutive bear bars 
3rd one is big so possible Sell Climax 
BO big enough for 2nd Leg Down 
Possible Measuring Gap 
Sell The Close bear trend 
Exit with limit order at 1st sell 
Breakeven on 1st sell 
1 pt profit on 2nd sell 
Bears sell close of bear bar 
Bears sell more at high of bull bar since 
not strong enough to reverse 3 big bear bars 
Also, it is 4 ticks above 1st entry 
Want 2nd sell to be at least 4 t icks above 1st sell 
Need that since want minimum scalp {4 ticks)

and false beliefs

@Albrooksall

Beginners lose most of their money 
by mistakes due to incorrect beliefs (Psychology) 
This video is on losing trades that beginners 
make by mistake, and how to avoid or fix them 
9~-
• 
I discuss h.ow to handle good trad·es t hat go bad 
(Market Risk, which means bad luck) in the videos 
on Losing When Good Trade Goes Bad 
Shorter video set because traders 
lose more money from mistak·es!

BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 2 
@Albrooksall

Beginners: Lose Because of Ignorance 
Ignorance does not mean low IQ 
Means lack of information 
Ignorance is barrier to making money 
Easy to fix by simply spending time 
being open to learning 
Sma·rt person can be ignorant of some 
ancient culture because he never studied it 
or thought about it 
9.1. 
o•

f9• 
+ 
Common ignorant beliefs that lead to losses: 
1. Perfect setups exist 
2. Noise exists 
3. Most of time, either only bulls or bears can make money

Slide 3 
@Albrooksall

Beginners: Bad Management Decisions 
Beginner sells, 
betting on 
Beginner buys 
Loses, despite good trade 
Does not want much risk so 
puts stop below signal bar 
Beginners are often wrong in picking entries, 
usually because they focus too much on risk 
and not enough on probability or reward 
Beginners often make bad management decisions 
Even when they know what the right thing to do is 
They conclude that this trade is an exception 
that requires them to do something else 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com

@Albrooksall

Confused Goals: Plan to Swing, but Trade Like Scalp 
Beginners often takes trade as swing, 
but manages as scalp 
Not enough risk when 50 - 60% probability 
of swing profit 
Upset by 2 bad follow-through bars 
Decides never really willing to risk to swing stop 
Tightens stop to scalper's stop 
Beginner buys 
PB in bull trend

below bottom of bull leg

to live in fear of big loss down at swing stop

Confused Goals: Plan to Scalp, but Trade Like Swing 
Beginner often takes trade as scalp, 
but manages as swing 
Too much risk for too little profit 
Beginner buys bull BO of Bull Flag, 
even though possible OT 
Upset that next bar is bear reversal 
.i.+,;,9,1,++ 
□· + 
Uses tight stop 
Would be embarrassed to lose 
Knew he bought high in weak rally, like a loser, 
but does not want to feel like a loser 
Lowers stop to swing stop to increase probability 
Exits with loss that is 4x bigger than his profit goal

Confused Goals: If Scalp, Manage Like a Scalp 
When entering as scalp, 
manage as scalp and take the scalp loss 
Cannot make money scalping if have big losers 
Uses tight stop 
Incorrectly believed this was a 
good Buy The Close (BTC) bull trend 
Did not realize it was Buy Vacuum test 
of high close of day and possible DT 
Did not see that it was possible Wedge Top

Market Cycle: 10°/o of Bars Are in Strong BO 
From the Market Cycle, traders know that 
Take profit at MM target 
10% of bars are in strong breakouts up or down 
Very positive Trader's Equation 
Strong enough bull BO on the open for 
High probability of MM based on Initial Risk 
70% chance of Measured Move {MM) up 
Only need reward of lx Actual risk 
based on the size of the BO

Buy close of consecutive big bull bar 
Closed on its high and above neck line of DB 
Could wait to buy close of follow-through bull bar

Slide 9 
@Albrooksall

Market Cycle: 90°/o of Bars Are in Channel or TR 
During the 10% of bars that are in a strong BO, 
usually difficult to make money trading 
in the opposite direction 
Bear sold at prior high 
If bear decided to stay short and 
try to manage to get a profit, 
he might sell more below 1st bear reversal bar 
Might change goal and just try to avoid loss 
Exit at average entry price

Avoid Big Loss: Always Follow These Rules 
40 - 60% of average reasonable trades 
will lose at least a little money 
(great scalpers win 90% of time, but difficult to do) 
Exit if reasonable signal in opposite direction 
If reversal fails, can always put position back on 
Exit immediately if clearly wrong, like if 
there is reversal with consecutive strong bars 
Sell OT Bear Flag 
Stop 
(]9 
Exit on close of follow-through 
after bull BO bar and 
4 consecutive bull bars 
Exit above bull reversal bar 
HL MTR 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 2 
@Albrooksall

Most Important Protection: Protective Stop in the Market 
The most important protection from 
a loss growing too large is a protective stop 
A stop protects you from yourself 
more than from the market 
Always have stop in the market, not in your head 
If you only have it in your head, 
you will quickly discover that it too often 
stays in your head, and never reaches the market 
The result is a huge loss that wipes out 
t he last 20 things you did well 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 3 
@Albrooksall

Beginner buys High 1 Bull Flag 
(PB i:n strong bull trend) 
Why does he not exit after big bear BO 
and bear follow-through bar? 
Why does he buy more on what 
he sees as a High 2 Bull Flag? 
We were genetically bred to be hopeful 
People long ago who were in trouble, 
but gave up, died, and so did their genes 
People long ago who remained hopeful, 
and never gave up, had a much better chance 
of passing their genes on to us 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 4 
@Albrooksall

Hard to Admit Wrong: Nobody Likes a Quitter 
Exiting with a loss is giving up 
Beginner who is in a bad trade, 
often ref uses to give up 
Often makes it worse by adding to bad trade 
Traders know it is better to be 
a live coward, than a dead hero 
The goal is to fight for money, not for principles 
Social stigma against quitting 
Bad to not keep trying 
Only lazy cowards give up

Never Lose Sight of Goal: Make Money 
Beginner works hard to save enough money to 
open a small account and start trading 
That money is symbolic of 
birth of new life as trader 
Greatest fear is early death 
Beginner focuses ent·irely on risk of 
Trader's Equation has 3 variables: 
Risk ( distance to stop) 
Reward (distance to profit target) 
Probability (the odds of making money 
before the stop rs hit) 
All 3 have to work together to create profit 
Avoiding risk is not enough 
losi·ng money, which represents the death of his dream 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com

Greatest Fear: Death (Blown Account) 
Beginner fears an early death as a trader, 
which means a ''blown account'' where the value 
falls below the necessary minimum for trading 
He wrongly assumes that death 
can only come from risk 
There are two other variables that can also 
cause him to lose his account 
Needs to wait to take profits that are 
big enough to make the Trader's Equation positive 
If low probability trade, must hold for big profit, 
or account will slowly die

We are human 
Even great traders mal<e mistakes, 
but are quick to fix them 
Learn what to do when you make a mistake

Biggest Losses: Scaling In, but Position Size Too Big

Since now bigger position, more dollars at risk 
Single costliest mistake beginner makes is 
not reducing position size 
when scaling in and stop is far 
a 6

increases probability of profit 
Only experienced traders should scale in

being too hopeful instead of objective, 
can get big loss quickly, 
and give back profit from last 10 wins 
They plan to scale in, 
but quickly realize their size is too big 
Their plan was unrealistic 
Must trade very small if scaling in

are due to mistakes, not bad luck 
Experts lose less because they get out quickly and 
are not emotionally attached

Rules: 3 Types of Protection for Every Trade 
For every trade, use these 3 types of protection: 
Always have a reasonable protective stop in 
the market, and trade small if it is far away 
Exit if reasonable opposite signal 
( does not have to be perfect or strong) 
Exit if there is a strong, opposite BO 
~+ 
Stop 
Wedge HH MTR 
Many bear bars 
Weak rally so probably bull leg in TR 
and therefore bear leg likely soon 
Good sell signal bar 
Stop 
3 consecutive bars closing on lows 
3rd bar big 
Reasonable context for reversal

Most Important Rule: No Mental Stops 
Need rules to minimize loss 
once trend evolves into TR, 
or reverses into opposite trend 
-- Trailing Stop 
Most important rule is to always 
have a stop order in the market 
to guarantee no more than an acceptable loss 
There are often 2 - 3 good choices for stop 
Trail stop after each new strong BO to new extreme

Appropriate Stop: If Wrong, Get out Early 
Even a great scalper will lose 10% of time 
Prevent losses from becoming too big 
by a I ways using rules that get you out early 
Always have a stop order in the market 
at an appropriate location 
Scalper buys close 
--

like a reversal with consecutive strong bars 
Stop above Measured Move up 
Scalper sells at prior high 
Stop

exit on close of reversal bar 
around breakeven
