@Albrooksall

Strong Trends: Eventual Disappointment 
Buy The Close bull trends and 
Sell The Close bear trends are common 
Each eventually has a disappointing bar or two 
that can lead to at least a minor reversal, 
and a possible TR or major reversal 
Most traders can avoid a loss 
if they manage their trades we11 
I discuss management in the videos on 
''Entering Late in Trends'' and 
will briefly discuss it again here 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 12 
@Albrooksall

Buy The Close: Always Has Final Trend Bar 
Once bull believes it is a Buy The Close bull trend, 
he buys one -or more closes 
If the bar following his buy is a strong bear bar, 
he suspects that he just bought the 
final bull trend bar 
Q 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 13 
@Albrooksall

Easiest Acceptable Management: Just Rely on Stop 
Easiest approach is to rely on stop 
As long as premise is still valid and stop not hit1 
trader holds onto his position 
For example, bull buys the close, 
but the next bar is bear bar and the next 2 - 3 bars 
did not allow him to get out breakeven 
If disappointed by the strength of the selloff, 
just get out breakeven on a test of the entry price 
(the highest close) 
Common cause of DT (or DB) 
t~¢ 
Stop 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 14 
@Albrooksall

Scale In: Increments Based on Size of Smallest Scalp 
Some bulls will scale in 1 - 3 times, 
with each one based on size of the smallest scalp 
Some scale in below low of prior bar, 
as long as each entry is at least the 
size of a scalp below the last buy 
Trade small enough so that if stop is hit, 
loss is no more than with any other trade 
t~¢ 
-- Stop 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 15 
@Albrooksall

Disappointment: Exit to Avoid Loss, Not to Make Profit 
If disappointed by the strength of the selloff, 
just get out breakeven on 1st buy and 
with a profit on the lower buys 
t~¢ 
-- Stop 
Scale in to increase probability of profit, 
or ch-ance of avoiding loss 
Only for experts because have to manage well 
One mistake can quickly undo 10 good trades 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 16 
@Albrooksall

Disappointed, but No PB: Get out Breakeven 
Some bulls will scale in only one time 
They wait for a PB that is at least 2 - 3 times 
bigger than a minimum scalp 
They then buy above the high of a bull reversal bar 
They only buy if the distance from the 1st entry 
is at least 2 - 3 times the size of a minimum scalp 
(not here) 
If the PB does not go deep enough, 
they will just hold their original position 
If disappointed by the selling, 
they will exit breakeven 
t~¢ 
-- Stop

Reasonable Entries: Usually Get Tested within 1 - 3 Bars 
When there is a reasonable entry, 
and then an immediate reversal 
for 1 or more bars, 
the entry usually gets tested 
If not within 1- 3 bars, 
then within 20 bars 
''Test'' means within 
1- 2 ticks or pips 
Disappointed trader usually 
can exit breakeven 
or with small loss 
Sell The Close bear trend 
Final entry was followed by b,ig bull bar 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 14 
@Albrooksall

When bears could not get out 
Breakeven on 1st attempt, 
they raised limit order 
Still could not get out, gave up, 
and resulted in rally 
When bears give up, odds favor 2 legs up 
Bears desperate for PB to buy back shorts 
with smaller loss 
Bulls want PB to buy, since expect 
bears will not sell for many more bars

Do Not Let Losses Grow: Can Always Buy Again Later 
If bought the high close and reverses down strongly, 
you missed a topping pattern and a sell signal 
Even if you still do not see it, 
get out because the market is telling you 
that you are wrong 
Do not get paralyzed by a need to see 
what you missed 
Just exit with a loss before it grows big 
T 
When bulls give up, 
odds favor a swing down and probably 
Ten Bars, Two Legs (TBTL) 
Can always buy again if 
immediately reverses back up 
Stop

Structuring a Trade: Needs to Make Sense 
The key to making money is knowing how to structure a trade 
Decide: 
Whether to buy or sell 
How much to risk (where to put stop) 
What is profit goal (reward) 
T 
' 
There is no one way to trade 
Goal of videos is to show many ways 
to structure mathematically 
reasonable trades

Structuring a Trade: Usually Can Buy or Sell 
Only 10% of bars are in strong BO 
When strong BO, only trade with the trend 
For 90% of bars, possible to structure 
profitable long and profitable short 
Buy Sell Climax, 
betting on 
bounce here, 
or soon 
Buy more below strong bull bar, 
betting weak bulls will exit

with profit on lower, 2nd buy

Higher Time Frame: Use It to Find Setup 
Most traders who do this, trade the 60 min chart, 
and look for setups on 5 min chart 
Skilled scalpers who trade 5 min chart, 
look for early entries on 1 min chart 
Leg 1 = Leg 2 
5 min chart 
Buy reversal above 
failed BO below 
Wedge Bottom 
Most traders should never trade 1 min chart 
Not enough time for good decisions, or 
for order placement 
1 min chart 
5 min entry 
Stop -
~ 
Take profits at 
below Wedge Top 
Buy 5 ticks or pips earlier on 1 min 
Small risk 
Good context for bulls

Lower Time Frame: Use It to Reduce Risk 
In strong BO, traders often enter in direction of 
trend, on close of each strong trend bar 
Skilled scalpers who trade 5 min chart, 
look for early entries on 1 min chart 
(If trading 60 min chart, can enter in 5 min chart) 
Bears sell 
the close 
5 min chart 
Bears can look at lower time frame, for 
earlier entries in direction of strong trend 
1 min chart 
Stop 
On 1 minute chart, 
if 3 - 4 consecutive strong bear bars, 
bears can sell close 
Sell BO below bear flag 
Bears sell above 
high of prior bar, 
betting reversals will fail

Use Market Cycle: Market in Never Ending Cycle 
Bull trend channel 
Bull BO above bear channel 
Trend BO, then TR 
Will be followed by BO in either direction 
Learn to recognize where market is in cycle 
Then, trade appropriately for that part of cycle

Bull Stairs: Experts Will Sometimes Also Short 
Only sell in bull channel, 
if enough room for scalps 
First shorts are limit orders 
Only for experts 
Only if stairs (PBs overlap BO points) 
Stop 
Sell more

to avoid loss or make small profit 
Disappointed by bull BO 
Exit entire trade breakeven 
at bottom of channel

TR: Buy Low Sell High Scalp (BLSHS) 
Take strong buy signals, and 
2nd entry buys 
Scalp 
Stop 
TR can begin while still in 
bear channel

Weak rally 
Exit with tiny profit, basically breakeven

Buy Wedge Bottom, 2nd entry buy, 
expecting 2 Legs Up 
If TR becomes too 
tight, stop trading

60% probability of X ticks/pips down, 
before X ticks/pips up

Buy zone is lower 1/3rd of TR

60% probability of X ticks/pips up, 
before X ticks/pips down

Eventually will be strong BO of TR, up or down 
New Market Cycle will begin again: BO, channel, then TR

10% of bars are BO, so only trade with trend 
90% of bars in channel or TR, so 40 - 60 rule

taking opposite sides

Low probability (40%) 
Buying after BO 
High probability (60% or more) 
Bigger risk, smaller reward 
(worse risk/reward) 
Small risk, big reward (good risk/reward)

Sell close in strong bear BO 
6 consecutive bear bars, 
.._..~~ 
closing below midpoints 
All trends constantly try to reverse, 
but these are all minor reversals 
Hard to hold short 
Minor reversal usually becomes PB, 
which is bear flag 
Learn to rely on your stop 
Don't scalp when you should swing 
Profits often end up much larger than 
seems possible 
Math is good if scalp in strong trade, 
but much better if swing 
Beginners often scalp, but are unable to sell again 
Miss 80% of trend

Default Targets: Reasonable Approach to Taking Profits 
Initial Risk 
2x Initial Risk 
3x Initial Risk 
Reasonable default plan for taking profits: 
Take half off at 2x Initial Risk 
Take 25% more off at 3x Initial Risk 
Swing balance, trail the protective stop, exit before close 
Many ways to take profits 
Good to have default plan 
Can change to targets based on 
support and resistance at any time 
Look for reason to exit 
near end of session

Reversal: Often Has One Final Test in Wrong Direction 
EU RUSO 5 min 
One side gives up 
Either get BO or reversal 
Stop 
Markets often have one strong move in wrong direction 
50% of stop runs fail because they are simply 
test of strength of 2 sides 
Bears sell above bars and at prior high, 
betting either TR or bull trap 
Expect at least leg down and 
possible bear trend

Strong Rally: Can Reverse Down Quickly 
EURUSD 5 min 
Stop 
Bears allow bulls to test up 
Weak bears exit above LH 
Strong bears sell at and above LH to make bulls give up

bears win, and market reverses down 
Bear BO is sign that bulls finally gave up

LL Major Trend Reversal (MTR): Needs Strong Bulls 
EURUSD 60 min 
Usually need strong bull leg with at least 5 bull bars, or 
weaker bull leg or TR with 10 or more bars 
(here, 12 bars in bull leg, so good Buying Pressure) 
Bulls strong enough to break above bear trend line and MA, 
and to create gap bar (put bar entirely above MA) 
Wedge bottom lasting 14 bars

Tight TR During Trend: What Follows? 
Traders make decisions based on the 
highest strong close in a bull trend 
(or lowest close in bear trend) 
This is not noise 
Market is providing important 
information about what comes next 
I will explain in this video 
T 
Remember, noise is not random 
and cannot exist 
It simply refers to reversals that are 
smaller than what you want to trade 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 2 
@Albrooksall

Missed Trading Huge Trend: Out of Fear? 
EURUSD 15 min 
Did you watch this and not sell? 
1.1400 
If so, probably out of fear that the stop was far away, 
which can cause a huge loss 
1
-
1 380 
Traders call this a ''FOMO'' trend 
(Fear Of Missing Out) 
Often creates Tight Channel, relentless trends 
because everyone is constantly selling small, 
fearful of a reversal, 
and afraid of missing out of a big move 
Beginners fear loss and miss great trades 
Understand what is happening and reduce the fear

Trend: Do Not Let Yourself Be Trapped Out 
EURUSD 15 min 
Traders are selling closes until 
market transitions out of its BO phase 
They know what to do if it reverses up 
Good way to not be trapped out of bear 
is to be ''Sell The Close'' bear 
Sell close of strong bear bar 
for scalps and swing trades

Trend: Likely to Continue for at Least a Few More Bars? 
EURUSD 15 min 
Or, are bars small with tails on bottom 
and bull bodies? 
If so, bears no longer confident 
Bears now trying to scalp or exit breakeven 
Bulls see weak bears and are buying 
After about 10 - 20 bars in a bear, 
are bears acting as if bulls are trapped? 
Is there a big bear BO? 
If so, bears selling aggressively for swing down

Buy The Close Bulls: In Strong Bull BO, Buy Closes 
EURCAD 15 min 
Bear doji, so reversal attempt 
Strong enough rally to expect 
at least one more leg up after this 1st PB 
Stop 
Buy The Close bulls will keep buying closes 
until no longer profitable, 
expecting at least brief 2nd Leg Up

EURUSD 5 min 
Highest 
CLOSE 
of day 
High of day 
Close is most important price 
Candle chart and line chart 
Line chart only shows one price every 5 minutes 
Ignores all other prices

It is a dot chart where dots 
are connected with line 
Only price visible on line chart

EURUSD 5 min 
Tight stop means lose less per trade, but lose more often 
Wider stop has higher probability of profit, but loss is bigger 
As long as stop is reasonable, results are same after 100 trades 
Risking 2 pips or 500 pips is unreasonable ~• 
Results in losing strategy 
O 
~ 
Choose what suits your personality better 
Some prefer high probability 
Others prefer small risk

EU RUSO 5 min 
Simplest approach is to buy at the market as soon as bar closes 
Some place a limit order to buy the close as soon as next bar 
begins, but sometimes do not get filled 
Clearly Always In Long 
Buy the close 
Choose at least 
one entry 
"Buy The Close" bulls buy closes of consecutive bull bars 
as long as bars continue to close on their highs 
Most traders buy once and then swing trade 
Some experts keep buying more, increasing their position 
They are scaling into a winning trade

Once Clearly Always In Long: Look to Buy NOW! 
EURUSD 5 min 
May not be a "safe", clear PB entry for long time 
As you wait for smaller risk, 
you miss best part of trend 
Once you think market cycle 
changed to BO and 
Always In Long, 
buy at least small position 
Can never be more than 60% certain 
Don't be paralyzed by 40% chance of loss 
60% is as good as it gets 
If you are a trader, TRADE! 
If cannot decide which entry to buy, or 
if taking too long to enter ... 
buy at market and use correct stop

EURUSD 5 min 
Remember Market Cycle 
Trend is made of two parts 
BO is strong trend 
Often get consecutive BOs 
and nested BOs 
Channel that follows 
is weaker trend 
Different computers will draw 
channel differently, but in all cases, 
the PBs are sign that 
BO is evolving into channel 
As soon as PB, 
BO phase has ended or is starting to end 
Channel phase has begun 
BO might resume at any time

When Extremely Big Bars: Do Not Trade 
USDJPY 5 min 
Do not trade when this extreme 
Only experts should trade when this unusual 
Bar is 278 pips tall, but 
typical bar is about 10 pips 
Their choices are to trade very small size 
(easy to do with Forex markets), 
trade ETF (here, EWJ), or 
buy calls on futures or ETFs

Once Evolves into TR: Trade It Like TR, Not Trend 
EURUSD 5 min 
BLSHS means Buy Low, Sell High, Scalp 
See videos on Trading In TRs 
for stop placement and 
how to trade 
"Sell" can be sell out of longs or sell to get short 
Exit longs on any reasonable sell signal 
"Buy" can be buy back shorts or to get long 
Exit shorts on any reasonable buy signal

EURUSD 5 min 
Market still Always In Long 
Any short is scalp, 
so not much profit compared to risk 
Early in TR, so most should 
concentrate on buy signals 
Hard to do two things at once (exit long and go short) 
If can do it profitably and comfortably, then reasonable 
Most expert traders do not like to reverse 
Hard to be objective about opposite trade 
without first exiting current trade 
and waiting for a bar or two

Many Ways to Sell: All Good So Choose One 
USDCAD 5 min 
--- Rely on stop 
If cannot decide, sell market 
Press bets and sell BO below swing lows 
Sell close of any strong bear bar 
Sell below prior bar (bear flag) 
Sell above prior bar, betting reversal will fail 
Sell close of any strong bull trend bar, 
betting bull BO will fail 
Strong bear BO so must sell 
Many ways to sell 
Choose one

USDCAD 5 min 
Risking 2 pips or 500 pips is unreasonable 
Results in losing strategy 
Choose what suits your personality better 
Some prefer high probability 
Others prefer small risk 
Tight stop means lose less per trade, 
but lose more often 
Wider stop has higher probability of profit, 
but loss is bigger 
As long as stop is reasonable, 
results are same after 100 trades

Every BO: Is at Least a Minor Climax 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Climax is an acceleration of a move up, or down 
Extreme change in behavior 
Every BO (bar with big body} is a climax 
Most BOs (climaxes) are minor, 
and are part of TRs, or channels 
Most are followed by minor trends, or minor reversals 
1 1180

V Tops and Bottoms: Are Almost Always Something Else 
EURUSD 5 min 
V top implies unexpected reversal, 
like on surprise news 
Every Buy Climax is test of resistance, 
even if not obvious 
Every Sell Climax is test of support 
Wedge 
Buy Vacuum Test of prior high 
Expanding Triangle (bad shape 
so most computers ignore) 
Almost always some other pattern with clues, before reversal

Strong trends rarely become opposite strong trends

usually minor reversal into TR or Bull Flag

after Buy Climax bar with tail 
When selling Buy Climax, goal is scalp 
If then forms MTR, goal can be swing trade

Tight Channels: How Do You Know If Tight? 
EU RUSO 5 min 
The more of these features present, the better it is to only buy: 
PBs last mostly 1- 3 bars 
Size of PB is less than 2 - 3 times as big as smallest scalp (10 pips) 
Size of PB is less than 2 times the size of average bar 
Sell setups using stop entries are mostly losers, 
even if strong sell signal bar 
Most PBs only 1/3rd to 1/2 of 
prior bull leg 
?f 
Gaps between PB and BO points 
1 
If bear sells above and 
TT' 
scales in with limit orders, 
ft 
losses offset gains 
PB only 14 pips 
Average bar 
about 12 pips tall

Lose Money If Sell Tight Bull Channel: Don't Fight Trend! 
Unlike Broad Channels, 
when in Tight Channels, 
never trade against trend 
PBs are too small for bears 
Bears will lose money 
Tight Bull Channels always form 
Wedge "tops" when bulls take 
partial profits 
Weak bears sell below 
Weak bulls exit below 
Bull trend ignores them 
?f 
and keeps going higher

Lose Money If Sell Tight Bull Channel: Don't Fight Trend! 
Beginner wants to trade, 
but wants small risk 
He knows that if he buys, stop is far and risk is big 
If he sells, stop is close and risk is small 
Looks for reasons to sell 
because stop is small 
Ignores tightness of channel 
and therefore low probability 
Trader's Equation has 
?f 
3 variables, and 
all are important 
Buy Climax, Failed BO above 
Parabolic Wedge, and LH in TR, 
Strong sell signal bar, 
2nd entry sell signal 
Wedge Top 
Do NOT sell since 
probably buyers below

Bear MC: Bull Flag or Endless PB and Reversal? 
EURUSD 5 min 
Reversal in bull trend is usually PB 
This means it is Bull Flag 
Often is Tight Bear Channel or Tight TR 
Look to buy, like on resumption up 
See videos on: 
• 
Tight Channels and Micro Channels 
• 
Pullbacks and Bar Counting 
• 
Major Trend Reversals 
Once it grows to 10 bars, 
traders begin to wonder if it is 
Endless PB and start of bear trend 
More likely if after Buy Climax, 
like Parabolic Wedge Top 
and if 3 - 5 consecutive bear bars 
Probability still favors Bull Flag 
unless strong bear BO and follow-through

First Reversal Down: Usually Is Minor 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Even if trader bought highest close at 
Buy Climax at top of Wedge, 
risk is small if he manages his trade well 
Rely on stop 
Bears broke below trend line of final leg up 
and below entire bull channel 
Tight Channel usually becomes TR before bear trend 
so bulls have time to exit 
with low probability of stop being hit 
No clear MTR or broad Wedge Top 
Reversal has 70% chance of being minor and 
being followed by test of top of bull trend

Unlike BO Phase: Bulls Sell above High (Take Profits) 
EURUSD 5 min 
Bulls still see as strong trend 
Take most of same buy setups as they do during BO 
One big difference 
In BO, they sometimes 
buy above prior high and buy 
strong bull closes at new highs 
In channel, they sell above prior high 
to take full or partial profits 
Then, look to buy again on PB 
Instead of buying strong close in BO, 
bulls take partial profits above high 
When range is big, bulls take profits 5 - 20 pips above

EURUSD 5 min 
Scale in bear risked 140 pips to make 10 pips 
How can that result in a positive Trader's Equation? 
Scaling in increases probability, 
but needs probability of about 95% to make money 
That is impossible for even most great traders 
Pays for great probability 
with bad risk/reward (RR)

That Is Worst Case: Structure Trade to Be Better 
EURUSD 5 min 
Expert only scales in if can structure the trade 
to have realistic Trader's Equation 
"Structure" means have a plan with several alternatives 
Too difficult for beginner 
Rarely ever lose 140 pips 
since bear will exit early if premise no longer valid 
and will not continue to add if premise now weak 
Many winners and losers are only 10 - 30 pips 
If winning more than 60%, can be very profitable

High Probability: Can Offset Bad RR 
EU RUSO 5 min 
In Broad Channel, expert trader can win 90% of these trades 
Average win might be about 30 pips 
If he takes 10 trades and has 9 wins x 30 pips, 
then 270 pips total profit 
and loses 1 x 140 pips= 140 pips 
In Tight Bull Channel, bears often disappointed and 
exit around breakeven, not at 1st entry 
Average win might only be 10- 15 pips 
Even if wins 90% of time, barely makes money 
Most great traders rarely short in Tight Bull Channel

More confident of higher prices 
More willing to swing more of longs, 
for possible 2 legs up and Measured Mo 
Bears selling new highs 
are losing money 
Some bears reverse 
to make up for loss 
Most bears saw how hard it was to make 
money by selling above last 2 highs 
Conclude bulls are strong 
Unwilling to continue selling new highs 
unless evolves into stairs., TR, or bear swing

Bears Losing Money: Better to Just Exit with Loss 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Problem for bears in Tight Bull Channel 
Hard to make much money 
Eventually stop trying 
Often have to scale in 
2 - 3 times so big risk 
Scalp, so small profit 
Usually better to just exit with loss and 
wait 1- 2 bars and decide on next trade 
Often get bull BO with follow-through 
Reversing is often only way to make up loss, 
but reversing is hard, even for experts

Buy When Tight Bull Channel: Many Ways 
EURUSD 5 min 
Channel has frequent PBs 
Can buy BO above swing high, 
so many opportunities to buy below the high 
but many bulls take partial profits there, 
unlike in strong bull BOs 
where bulls buy there 
Buy below bar, especially if weak sell setup (like bull body) 
Bet bears will fail and have to buy back shorts 
Buy PB to support, like moving average or prior high 
Buy PB to support, like BO point or 50% PB 
Buy BO of bull flag, especially if bull signal bar 
Scale in 10 - 20 pips below high or earlier entry

if use swing stop, especially if can scale in

EU RUSO 5 min 
Bull trend, always reasonable to swing part or all 
Trend, so probability of making reward 
equal to Actual Risk is 60% 
when using correct stop 
lx Initial Risk,15 pips 
lx Actual Risk, 
but only 6 pips 
------ Actual Risk is 1 pip below low of PB 
Initial Risk is 1 pip below low of bull leg 
To have positive Trader's Equation (a good trade), 
minimum profit target is lx Actual Risk 
as long as at least 10 pips 
When trend is strong, many traders 
take 1st profit at lx Initial Risk 
as long as at least 10 pips

Minimum Profit: Rarely Is Best Choice 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Good math to scalp part at lx or 2x Actual or Initial Risk 
and swing the rest 
lx Initial Risk,15 pips 
lx Actual Risk, 
but only 6 pips 
Best math is to hold part or all, until at price where 
there are 2 or more reasons for significant resistance 
Then, exit most or all either with limit order, or 
with stop as market reverses down

Once Becomes TR: Stop Trading Like Trend 
EU RUSO 5 min 
After a trend lasts about 20 bars, 
look for signs that it might 
evo Ive into a TR 
Small TR, but 1st pause in strong bull 
usually becomes bull flag 
TR late in bull so stop trading like bull trend 
Probability of higher prices now no longer 60% 
50% chance of bull trend resumption 
50% chance of trend reversal into bear 
Here, the bears reversed the bull trend

Tight Channels: How Do You Know If Tight? 
EURUSD 5 min 
Gaps between PB and BO points 
If bull buys below and 
scales in with limit orders, 
losses offset gains 
Average bar about 10 pips tall 
11300 
1.1280 
1.1260 
Biggest PB only 20 pips 
1.1240 
The more of these features present, the better it is to only sell: 
PBs last mostly 1- 3 bars 
Size of PB is less than 2 - 3 times as big as smallest scalp {10 pips) 
Size of PB is less than 2 times the size of average bar 
Most PBs only 1/3rd to 1/2 of prior bear leg 
Buy setups using stop entries are all losers, even if strong buy signal bar

Buy Signal Bar in Tight Bear Channel: Sellers Above 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Beginner wants to trade, 
but wants small risk 
He knows that if he sells, stop is far and risk is big 
If he buys, stop is close and risk is small 
Looks for reasons to buy 
because stop is small 
Sees good buy signal bar 
and Nested Wedge Bottom 
Ignores tightness of channel 
and therefore low probability 
Trader's Equation has 3 variables, 
and all are important 
Do NOT buy since 
probably sellers above

Scaling into Longs: Only for Experts 
USDCAD 5 min 
Bulls got out breakeven on 1st entry 
Made 10 pips on 2nd buy 
Made 20 pips on 3rd 
Total profit of 30 pips 
Terrible strategy for most traders 
They will never trade as small as they should 
They will worry too much about risk and 
not scale in or exit correctly 
THEY WILL LOSE! 
If 40 pip stop was hit, bull would have lost 
40 pips on 1st entry 
30 pips on 2nd short 
20 pips on 3rd 
10 pips on 4th 
Total loss of 100 pips

After Each New Strong Bear BO: Trail Stops 
EURJPY 5 min 
~
-Stop 
After each new strong BO, 
especially if to a new low, 
lower stop to above its high 
Even when scalping, more likely to make money 
if use swing stop, especially if can scale in 
No matter where you enter in a leg, use same stop 
Any entry in a leg is just one of many ways 
If not certain BO is strong, 
can tighten or use prior stop 
to enter the same trade

EURJPY 240 min 
Broad Bull Channel is Tight Bull Channel 
on higher time frame

better to just manage the trade 
on the daily chart

Most traders trading this chart would 
trade legs up and down, not buy and hold

Broad Channels: How Do You Know If Broad? 
EURUSD 5 min 
The more of these features that are present, 
the more traders will both buy and sell 
Legs up and down lasting mostly 5 or more bars 
Size of average leg is at least 3 times bigger than 
smallest scalp (10 pips) 
Size of leg is at least 2 - 3 times 
the size of average bar 
PB 50 pips 
Average bar 
about 10 pips tall

Broad Channels: Why Not Common on 5 Min Chart? 
EURUSD 5 min 
Usually can not clearly form Broad Channel 
on 5 minute in one session, 
or on any chart unless there are at least 100 bars 
To believe it is bull channel and not TR, 
usually need at least 3 legs up and 3 legs down 
Possible HL in 3 day Broad Bull Channel 
It is also a TR and an early bear trend 
Not enough information for 
high probability trading

EU RUSO 5 min

over 2 - 3 days ( here, 7 days)

can contain bear trends

based on patterns created today or 
that started yesterday

EURUSD, 5 min. 7 days in Broad Bull Channel 
Broad Bull Channels form TRs 
TR contains topping patterns 
TR constantly tries to reverse down 
If PB falls below HL or trend line, 
TR might be starting 
Reversal attempt breaks below old trend line, 
creating broader, flatter channel 
Eventually evolves into big TR (here, still in channel) 
All channels become broader and flatter 
with each new TR and as each TR grows

Bull Trend Needs Hls: But Do Not Let Stops Get Hit 
EURUSD 5 min 
In Broad Bull Channel, 
In bull trend, bulls trail stops to below most recent 
HL after a strong BO to or near a new high, 
most do not let stop get hit, and 
exit or go short on reversal down 
or any strong BO above a bear channel 
As long as making Hls, then 
still bull trend 
Some bulls will buy below bull leg for scalp, 
since TR more likely than bear trend

traders use size of prior BOs to manage trades

BO point 
Measuring Gap 
PB stayed a little above prior high

Bears short at or above prior high and scale in higher for scalps 
Bears use size of prior BOs to determine where to put stops

Bears Take Profits: Need Minimum Scalp 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Minimum scalp size is 10 pips 
Minimum reward is 1- 2x actual risk 
Actual risk is 5 pips 
2x Actual risk is 10 pips 
When 2x actual risk is only 10 pips, 
that profit is probably too small for 
channel that is about 70 pips tall 
Try for bigger profit: 
Fixed number of pips, like 20 - 50 pips 
Around middle of channel (50% PB) 
Hold for swing down and exit on reversal up

Scale In: To Make Small Profit or Reduce Loss 
EURUSD 5 min 
Some bears scale in higher 
Increases probability of profit, but increases risk 
Sell more at fixed interval like 10 - 50 pips 
Sell more at 50% between high and stop 
Sell more at new high 
Sell more as rally turns back down 
If scaled in, exit all 
Reduced or avoided loss 
Trade small enough so risk not big 
Trading small reduces profit 
To get increased probability, 
must accept worse risk/reward

Strong Bear BO Attempts: Reverse up Quickly 
EURUSD 5 min 
Because bulls expect deep PBs and 
often wait to buy until in the bottom half, 
there can be a lack of buyers in the upper half 
This often creates a sell vacuum 
and a strong bear BO 
Bears think they won 
Stop 
Once the bear BO reaches the buy zone 
(bottom 2/3rds), bulls buy aggressively and 
overwhelm the bears, and create another HL

Wedge

Broad Channels: Why Not Common on 5 Min Chart? 
AUDUSD 5 min 
Usually can not clearly form Broad Channel 
on 5 minute in one session, 
or on any chart, unless there are at least 100 bars 
1 
2 
To believe it is bear channel and not TR I 
usually need at least 3 legs up and 3 legs down 
Possible OT Bear Flag in 6 day Broad Bear Channel 
It is also a TR and 
a possible DB and HL MTR, and then a bull trend 
Not enough information for high probability trading

EURUSD 15 min, Broad Bear Channel 
Broad Bear Channels form TRs 
TR contains bottom patterns 
TR constantly tries to reverse up 
Reversal attempt breaks above old trend line, 
creating broader, flatter channel 
Eventually evolves into big TR (here, still in channel) 
If PB rallies above LH or trend line, 
TR might be starting 
All channels become broader and flatter 
with each new TR and as each TR grows

Stop below support, 
like Measured Move

take profits on reversal down 
with small profit or loss 
{here, tried to exit above prior high)

If Stop Not Hit and Premise Still Valid: Bulls Can Scale In 
EU RUSO 60 min 
Bulls bought at prior low 
If use wide stop, can scale in lower 
Scale in increases probability of 
profit, but increases risk 
Trade small enough so risk not big 
Trading small reduces profit 
To get increased probability, 
must accept worse risk/reward 
Buy more at fixed interval like 50 pips 
Buy more as reverses up 
If still in trade, buy more at 50% 
between 1st entry and stop

Base Stop: On 2x Size of Biggest Prior BO

Creates lack of sellers in the bottom half

This often creates a buy vacuum 
and a strong bull BO 
Bulls think they won 
bears sell aggressively and overwhelm the bulls, 
and create another LH

Confusion: Not as Good or Bad as It Appears 
Strong bear BO so possible bear trend 
1st rally will probably be sold for test back down 
Beginners believe huge bear trend 
Skilled traders think 60% chance of 
Measured Move (MM) down 
and 40% chance of reversal up 
Strong bear BO so bear trend or TR likely 
Bull trend unlikely 
If TR, can have deep PB (rally) 
Strong reversal up, but still LH 
1st reversal down will probably 
be bought for test back up 
Big down, big up, so big confusion 
Disappointed bears and bulls 
Skilled traders now believe 60% 
chance of more TR

80°/o Rule: Failed BO and Then Reversal Is Common 
EURUSD 60 min 
Reversals and PBs after only 1- 3 bars 
Lack of consecutive big strong trend bars 
in bull swings and bear swings 
Bad follow-through after big trend bars 
Markets have inertia 
They are resistant to change and tend to 
continue what they have been doing 
In TR, 80% of BOs 
up and down fail

~1,1 
---~'·11t,ij1 
Failed BOs above prior highs and below prior lows 
Traders betting on failures make money

and waiting for better

only brief consecutive strong bull bars

Bulls and Bears: Both Buy in Bottom Half, Sell in Top Half 
EURUSD 60 min 
Bulls sell to take profits in the top half, 
where bears are shorting 
Bears buy to take profits in the bottom halt 
where bulls are buying 
With both bulls and bears buying low, selling high, 
and taking quick profits {like scalping for 20 pips), 
the market continues sideways

LCM: Limit Order Market, Betting against 80s 
EURUSD 60 min 
A stop entry order is a bet 
on a BO and a trend 
In TTR, scalping with stop orders lose so often 
that it is hard to make money with stop orders 
TRs contain 
many Tight TRs

Trend Bars: Expect Bad Follow-Through 
EURUSD 60 min 
If a bull BO needs 1 more bar 
to become clearly Always In Long, 
bears will sell the close, betting on 80% rule and 
betting against the next bar being a bull bar 
Bears sell any 
strong bull close 
near top of TR 
If a bear BO needs 1 more bar 
to become Always In Short, 
bulls will buy the close, betting on 80% rule and 
betting against the next bar 
being a bear bar 
Bulls buy any 
strong bull close 
near bottom of TR

Scaling In: Increases Probability, but Adds Risk 
EURUSD 60 min 
Bears will sell above a prior high 
when in the top half of the range 
Scaling in is not for beginners 
Stop--
Most should wait to sell more below bear bar 
since higher probability 
Then tighten stop to above bear bar 
Here, no bear sell signal bar 
Minimum scalp 
is 10 pips 
Last BO was 16 pips 
Sell more 
10 pips higher

Scale In: Increase Probability of Profit 
EURUSD 60 min 
== Stop 
Strong bull BO above TR 
and strong follow-through 
Most never plan to let wide stop be hit 
Usually better to exit with loss 
when believe trade is bad 
Exit short on close of follow-through bar 
They would consider selling again if BO fails 
Sell failed bull BO, 2nd entry 
Here, Micro DT and big bear bar 
Exit breakeven on 1st short 
and with 37 pips profit on 2nd short

Scale In: To Make Profit or Reduce Loss 
EURUSD 60 min 
Traders scale in to increase probability of profit, 
or to reduce size or probability of loss 
Pay for this with increased risk so trade small 
== Stop 
Best choice for 
/ 
scale in bears 
< 
( 
When believe trade is bad, 
some scale in to reduce or prevent loss 
If bear sold at prior high, quickly 
became worried that leg up was too strong 
Possible bull trend 
instead of 2nd Leg Trap in TR 
Some hold and scale in, hoping to reduce loss 
Sell below bear bar closing near low 
Exit at midpoint of both entries 
or at 1st entry with profit on 2nd 
Whenever there is a surprise BO against 
a reasonable trade, a scale in trader has 
an 80% chance of getting out at least breakeven

Wide Stop: Protect against Black Swan Catastrophes 
EURUSD 60 min 
== Stop 
Purposes of wide stop are 
to give time to make decisions and 
to protect against Black Swans 
Trader always plans to exit 
before wide stop is hit 
Wide stop protects against Black Swans 
Low probability events that can create huge loss 
Internet or computer problems 
Exchange, broker and software problems 
Personal health and family emergencies

Scale In: To Increase Chance of Profit 
EURUSD 60 min 
Some bulls will buy more lower 
Increases chance of profit 
Reduces size and chance of loss 
Increases risk so trade small enough 
so that risk is acceptable 
Minimum scalp is 10 pips 
legs are more than 50 pips 
Scale in 20 - 50 pips lower 
i.1'!~1

Most add on only once 
Some add on 2 - 3 times, 
but trade small size

TR: Can Be Tight Trading Range (TTR) 
EURUSD 5 min 
TR is horizontal channel 
Like all channels, can be broad or tight 
When broad, can enter with stop, market, 
or limit orders, and can scalp or swing 
When tight, little profit potential 
Can only scalp and only enter with limit orders 
Never scalp with stop orders since most are losers 
Some use swing stops and scale in 
Most should wait 
Forex often enters TTR 
in between sessions 
A TTR is a Limit Order Market

1 0985 
1 0980

~r,1,ottri. ... ,o 
Range is too tight to make enough 
1096~ 
money to compensate for effort and risk 
Wait for broader range or BO

EURUSD 5 min 
To scalp in TTR, need: 
Most bars more than size of scalp (10 pips) 
Height of TR at least either 3 times size of average bar, or 
3 times as bigger than smallest scalp (10 pips) 
Most should not even trade with limit orders

EU RUSO 5 min 
If going to trade in tight range, use mostly limit orders 
Sell with limit order near top and scalp 
Sell strong bull close or above high 
If small bear bar at top, 
rarely can sell with stop (not here) 
Want to exit with 
10 - 20 pips profit 
Here, range too tight 
No winners 
Buy with limit order near bottom and scalp 
Buy strong bear close or below low 
If small bull bar at bottom, 
rarely can buy with stop 
Sell at prior high 
or strong bull close 
Exit above bull bar 
around breakeven 
Buy above bull bar 
Buy below low 
near bottom 
or strong bear close 
Exit around 
breakeven

EURUSD 5 min 
Trade like any other Limit Order Market 
Buy bear strength in the bottom half 
Sell bull strength in the top half 
Scalp out in middle third 
Buy 10 pips below prior low 
or scale in 10 pips lower 
Buy reversal up 
Many scale in, scalp, and use wide stops 
Sell strong bull close 
Sell at prior high 
Sell 10 pips above prior high 
or scale in 10 pips higher 
(not big enough BO here) 
Sell reversal down 
Sell above weak Bull Flags 
Buy at prior low 
Buy strong bear close 
Buy below weak Bear Flags

EU RUSO 5 min 
TR traders Buy Low, Sell High, and Scalp 
until strong BO with follow-through 
Bet on continued TR and failed BO 
~ 
Bulls buy bear BO, 
betting on reversal up 
Some scale in 
Bulls give up and seJI out of longs 
Bears sell, betting on bear trend and 
Measured Move down 
Only enter with limit orders if can make 
quick decisions and get out when wrong 
Bears sell bull BO, 
betting on reversal down 
If BO bar is big, 
closes near low, 
and has follow-through, 
traders believe TR has ended 
and bear trend has begun

BO: Can Be Made of Channel and Many Small Bars 
EURUSD 5 min 
Every TR eventually ends with a trend 
50% chance of bull or bear trend 
BO can be one big trend bar or 
a series of smaller trend bars 
See videos on Trading Breakouts 
Once becomes a trend, stop trading like a TR

Most traders trade only 
one of three sessions: 
• Asia: 3 pm - midnight PST 
• Europe: 11 pm - 7 am PST 
• USA: 5 - 6 am - noon PST 
Sessions overlap 
Trading can increase during overlap

3 Parts of Each Session: Useless for Forex Traders 
EURUSD 5 min

Dividing the session into 3 parts is helpful for Emini traders, 
but usually not helpful for Forex day traders 
Forex markets have fewer and smaller swings 
than stock index futures contracts on most days 
And, Forex sessions tend to end quietly without a final surge 
If the Emini has 3 - 4 legs or swings, 
the Forex markets might have only 1 or 2 
All that matters is that the price action can change at any time, 
from trend to Trading Range (TR) or from TR to trend 
Be alert and change how you trade as the market changes 
BrooksTradingCourse.com

3 Parts of Each Session: Useless for Forex Traders 
EU RUSO 5 min 
~ 
~v 
V 
15 pip range, 
Emini 5 min 
Only 1.5 times size 
of minimum scalp 
16 point range, 
16 times size of 
minimum scalp 
Many bars 
only 3 pips tall 
,i,Q 
Bad trading 
Emini 
Final 3rd of day 
Good trading 
IE 
200100 
2IXIJ(I) 
Forex markets usually enter Tight Trading Ranges (TTR) 
after around 11 am PST, and usually do not move much at 
the end of the session 
Unlike the Emini, where the swings often get big again at the 
end of the day 
11 am is the approximate end of the Forex trading session 
(the end of the "Final 3rd" , which rarely ever clearly exists) 
It is also when stock index futures begin their Final 3rd, 
and usually have many good trades left

Between Sessions: Usually No Gaps 
EU RUSO 5 min 
9 trading days 
More than 20 sessions (3 sessions/day) 
No gaps 
No significant gaps when market is closed 
Not enough news during brief time 
when closed to create gap when opens again 
Price is usually close to unchanged

a trader is a day trader 
Day traders use a 15 minute or smaller time frame ·-
chart. Most examples here are on 5 minute charts ·-

The Open: What Is It When There Are 3 Sessions? 
EURUSD 5 min 
There is often an Opening Range, 
which is an early TR, before there is a BO 
into the 1st swing of the session 
' 
--
"'· 
... 
-
••

p,,,91

~-
Sometimes there is an Opening BO 
up or down instead of an Opening Range 
- .. .. ... - . . -
~ 
.. .. .. .. .. - .. -- .. 
-----
It is the time from when the market opens 
until the end of the i sl swing up or down 
.. -- - .. -- -· - .. -- .. ... .. -- -- - --
No matter how strong an Opening BO is, 
there is a 50% chance it will reverse 
.. - ...... - .............. . 
-----
If the market continues sideways all day, 
the open is the initial TR that 
failed to have a successful BO

I will discuss this in detail in 
the Scalping Videos 
Sell with limit order 
( or market order) 
Range contains Broad Micro Channels 
Bodies are mostly big 
Bars do not overlap much 
Traders can enter with stops or limit orders 
When tails are prominant, several bars overlap, 
and reversal come every 1- 3 bars, 
most should wait 
Scale in higher 
Sell with limit order 
Buy with limit order 
Extreme scalpers who can make quick decisions 
will enter with limit or market orders, 
scale in, and scalp: BLSHS !

@Albrooksall

Most Important Rule: Be Prepared for Anything! 
The open often has surprising moves that seem 
too big or irrational to beginners 
Experts do not care 
If the market is going up, they buy because it 
might go above all "reasonable" resistance 
If the market is going down, they sell because 
it might go below all "reasonable" support 
Trade what is there, 
Never have an opinion 
about what the market 
should do or not do 
even if you do not understand why 
it is doing what it is doing

BrooksPriceAction.com

@Albrooksall

"Low" probability moves are common 
Context (what happened yesterday or last week) 
is less important on the open 
However, every reversal takes place 
at support or resistance that was created 
during prior sessions 
Knowing where it is helps when looking for trades, 
but it is not necessary, since the open has much 
more to do with today than with prior sessions

The Middle: Only Important If It Is Different from the Open 
Bull trend on open became bear trend 
,. 
I 
ij\~~ I 
Market cycle has 3 possible behaviors: 
Bull trend, bear trend, and TR 
Bear trend became bull trend, 
then TTR and Broad Bear Channel 
Most days change behavior after 1- 3 hours 
Traders always need to be ready 
to change how they are trading 
TR became bigger TR, 
then bull trend 
TR became bear trend 
Bear trend began as OT in TR 
TR continued as TR 
Bear trend became TR

Trend from the Open: Will It Continue? 
Is the trend strong enough 
to continue all day? 
Only 10% of days have 
no significant pause or reversal 
..t••.ar,_ 
~ 
90% of trends are followed by 
opposite trend (TR day) or TR 
(possible resumption or reversal) 
Small 11orderly" Pullbacks {PBs) 
No big bear trend bars, 
which can lead to a Climactic Reversal 
Is the trend so strong 
that it is unsustainable? 
ilf+;I._..~ 
Parabolic 
Wedge 
Is it climactic and likely to end? 
Consecutive Sell Climaxes 
Small ii Final Bear Flag 
Are there strong trend bars, 
but weak follow-through bars? 
More common when trend is leg in TR 
TR has at least one leg down and one leg up, 
and either can come first 
If initial trend looks weak, be prepared for it 
to evolve into TR or opposite trend 
after 2 - 4 hours

Middle of Day: Test of Support or Resistance 
EURUSD 5 min 
Important function of middle of day 
is to test support or resistance 
Reaction from there determines direction of 
end of day 
This might be 
the consensus choice and 
therefore a magnet later in day 
24 hour market and no exchange 
so never an exact opening time or price for US session 
Since less certainty, fewer computers rely on it 
Less important than for stocks and futures 
.,,..,-, 
Look for strong trend bar that is followed by other 
trend bars up or down at around 5 to 6 am PST

TR: Test of Support or Resistance at End of Middle 3rd 
EURUSD 5 min 
When day is TR, 
and open of session is in middle 3rd of session's range, 
usually get test back to open in 2nd half of day 
After testing the open, 
day stayed there for rest of US session

After trend, if PB breaks channel 
and forms gap bars, 
probable attempt at MTR after trend resumes

Sign of strong bulls 
Weak rally and now dojis 
at end of day

EURUSD Daily chart 
At the end of day, look for test of 
daily support and resistance 
H, L, C of yesterday or H, L of any 
recent day 
Closed below high of 2 days ago 
and above yesterday's high

EURUSD 5 min 
Can still often get 10 - 20 pip scalp 
After strong trend, often enters tight 
that can take 1- 2 hours to reach target 
TR at end of session 
Buy above any bull bar near bottom of TTR, 
This is Dead Money, which means you 
hoping for 30 pip rally to test of top of range 
get very little return on your money 
TTR (Triangle) so probability close to 50%,

EURUSD 5 min

so risk was less than 14 pips 
for all of these buy entries

probability about 50%, risk 14 pips, reward 30 pips

Strong Trends: Suddenly Stop at End of Session 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Many dojis, probable bull leg in TR 
Sell reversal down at top of channel 
Nested Wedge 
Good sell signal bar 
Strong trend often suddenly stops 
and enters TTR at 
end of middle 3rd of session 
Once 3 - 5 small dojis appear, look to exit 
Probably entering TTR for rest of session 
Ok to take profit since probably little profit remaining, 
especially if not worth waiting 2 - 3 hours 
Can hold short for 2nd Leg Down 
and exit at end of session 
Small dojis so usually take hours 
and results in only small profit (Dead Money)

EU RUSO 5 min 
If Buy The Close rally or Sell The Close selloff 
comes at end of session, exit on reversal, 
which can go far and fast 
When comes at end of session, usually lasts 
only 4 - 6 bars and then becomes TR or reverses 
Sell The Close bear trend 
Try to sell 1st or 2nd Sell The Close bars 
because usually brief trend 
Do not try to take every sell

Buy The Close: Can Reverse Quickly 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Buy The Close bull trend at end of Asia session 
Small bars 
Tails 
Wedge, top of channel 
Better to not buy closes 
If bought closes or if long from earlier, 
exit below bear bar or 3 - 5 pips below bull bar

Day Trading: Only 25°/o of Days Have Swing Trades 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Swing trade typically means: 
Reward at least 2x risk 
At least 2 legs 
Usually 10 or more bars 
Profit of 20 - 50 pips 
Don't exit if minor reversal 
Only 25% of days have reasonable 
swing trades for day traders 
Most day traders are therefore scalpers, 
but most are always ready to swing trade

Bull BO at Start of European Session: Need Follow-Through 
EURUSD 5 min 
4 consecutive bull bars 
Bull body on follow-through bar after BO bar 
Always In Long 
60% chance 1st reversal down will be bought 
Bulls buy close of BO bar or of follow-through bar 
Small follow-through bar, tails on top of bars 
Not as strong as consecutive big bull bars 
closing on their highs 
Warning of possible TR or even reversal down 
Other bulls place limit order to buy low of prior bar 
and keep raising it until filled 
6 bar bull Micro Channel and strong BO bar 
80% chance of enough buyers below 
Bulls can make a profit or avoid a loss 
if they can scale in and exit appropriately

Disappointing Follow-Through: Possible Reversal 
EURUSD 5 min 
Bulls disappointed by small follow-through bar 
and bar with bear body 
Many Buy The Close bulls tried to get out 
breakeven by selling test of high close 
Bulls who bought low of prior bar 
now have bear entry bar 
Now believe TR more likely than bull trend 
Bulls will scale in 10 pips lower 
Try to get out breakeven on 1st buy 
and with scalp on lower buy

jt

Often Searches for Direction: Be Patient 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Bulls buy reversal up or above bull bar 
DB with BO bar 
Micro DB 
Bulls willing to hold for swing, 
but need strong BO above highest close 
If no strong bull BO, bulls will give up and exit 
Bears will sell 
5 bars without bear body, but small bull bars 
Disappointed bulls ready to get out quickly

Strong Bear BO: Possible Swing Down 
EURUSD 5 min 
-
---==---- Stop 
Reversal down from high of day forming DT 
Could not close above earlier highest close 
Rejecting this price level as too high 
------Stop 
Aggressive bears sell below prior bar, betting on OT 
Some bears use wide stop beyond MM 
Scale in on rally above prior high 
Higher probability, but greater risk 
Bears sell close of outside down bar or 
below its low 
Possible TR so not yet high probability sell 
Bears sell at market as reversal bar is forming, 
or sell on smaller time frame

Strong Bear BO: Possible Swing Down 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Bears sell BO below neck line of OT 
Bears sell close of big bear BO bar 
Big bear bar, close on (or near) low 
Closed below all prior bars of session 
Give-up Bars mean final bulls are selling 
Bears urgently selling as bars are forming 
Traders see as very important bar

Bottom, Then 2nd Bottom: Possible High 4 Buy Setup 
EU RUSO 5 min 
2 
Small 2nd Leg Down 
High 2 bottom then another High 2 bottom, 
so High 4 bottom 
Around Measured Move 
Consecutive Complex Bottoms 
( each with at least 2 legs) 
70% chance of TR soon 
Swing bears will begin to take profits and 
wait 10 - 20 bars before selling again 
6 dojis in Tight Trading Range (TTR), TR likely soon 
TTR, stop trading until BO

Wedge Top, but Tight Channel: Don't Sell 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Weak rally at end of Europe session 
Wedge Bear Flag 
Double Top Bear Flag, test of gap 
Tight Wedge channel so low probability sell 
Limit Order Market (LOM), 
but range is too tight to make 10 pips profit 
Better to wait for BO up or down

many traders then either scalp 
or wait for next trend

When Trending: Scalp with Swing Stops 
EURUSD 60 min 
Scalping in the direction of a strong trend, 
can be a profitable strategy 
In trends, most scalpers take very few entries 
Make less profit in trend than swing tr ders 
Since scalping means small reward, 
need high probability to offset bad risk/reward 
Can make consistent profit only 
if take high probability scalps, which are few 
Can only increase the probability by 
using swing stop, and usually by also scaling in

EURUSD 60 min 
These are times when scalping has the best chance 
to be profitable, and when swing traders are 
usually not making money 
The tighter the TR, 
the less chance swing traders have to make money 
If swing trader continues to rely on his stop, 
often has to wait 20 or more bars for a profit

Stairs: Scalpers Will Bet against BOs 
When a PB overlaps a BO point, 
the trend is in a stairs pattern 
-
BO point 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 9 
@Albrooksall

Stairs: Scalpers Will Bet against BOs 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 10 
@Albrooksall

Bear Stairs: Buy at Prior Low and Scale in Lower 
Trends tend to become weaker as they progress 
Once a stairs pattern develops, 
traders still bet it will continue, 
but expect start of transition into TR 
-
Bull scalpers will buy at the prior low 
If the BO falls for the size of a scalp, 
they will buy more 
Once the PB gets above the BO point, 
they get out breakeven on their 1st buy 
and with a profit on their lower buy 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 11 
@Albrooksall

Stairs: If Quick Profit, Take It and Do Not Wait to Scale In 
If the market goes immediately to their target, 
they exit with a scalp profit, 
without having to scale in 
To learn more about scaling in above prior highs 
and below prior lows for scalps, see videos on: 
Trading Tight Bull Channels 
Trading Broad Bull Channels 
Trading Tight Bear Channels 
Trading Broad Bear Channels 
Trading in Trading Ranges 
Losing Because of Mistakes

Emini 5 min 
Most scalpers use 1- 15 min charts 
On most days, average range 
of 5 min Emini bar is 6 - 10 ticks 
50 - 100% bigger than minimum scalp of 1 point

1 Min Chart: Most Should Avoid since Many Mistakes 
Whenever a trader improves his risk/reward, 
he pays for it with slightly lower probability 
Makes 1 min chart less reliable than 5 min 
Bar can go from a bad setup to a great one, 
or visa versa, in a second 
1 min

Slide 17 
@Albrooksall

1 Min Chart: Often Big Move in Final Seconds 
Institutions often create a tail, or erase a tail 
in the final second of the bar, 
to lower your probability (increase your mistakes) 
Surprises and confuses beginner 
Makes rushed, bad decisions 
Often quickly places order, 
then immediately decides he made mistake 
Often places order 1- 2 pips above, or below 
where he intended 
More likely institutions will take your money 
1 min 
Most traders cannot consistently make money 
if chart has more than 20 bar/hour 
If use tick or volume chart, 
make sure to create one 
with no more than 20 bars per hour

1 Min Chart: Most Should Avoid since Many Mistakes 
Looks easy, but beginners often 
buy too early above bad 1 min setups, and 
don't use wide enough stops 
Lose money buying in bull trend! 
Expert probably would not take that buy 
If he did, he would use wide stop 
He might scale in lower 
1 min 
Beginner takes low probability buy 
on 1 min chart, looking for 10 pip scalp 
Wedge, good signal bar, 
but Tight Channel so minor reversal 
Stopped out for 4 pip loss

10 Pip Scalp: Easier If Most Bars Are More Than 10 Pips Tall 
EURUSD 5 min 
Realistic minimum size for Forex scalp is 10 pips 
Difficult to make a living as a scalper 
Almost impossible if reward is less than 10 pips 
Spread becomes disproportionately too big 
Need to win 90% of trades to be profitable 
L

Small Bars: Difficult to Make Money Scalping 
EU RUSO 5 min 
This box is 10 pips tall: I 
8 bars in about 100 were 10 pips tall 
The range was about 50 pips tall 
When 90% of bars are less than 10 pips tall, 
difficult to make money on this time frame 
This is about as small a day 
that any scalper should trade 
If bars or legs are smaller, wait

Scaling In: Increases Probability of Profit 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Buy at prior low, betting on failed bear BO 
Buy more 10 pips lower, or on reversal up 
Risk about 30 pips from 1st entry, 
about 3 times bigger than minimum scalp 
Exit at 1st entry 
Breakeven on 1st entry 
10 pips profit on 2nd entry 
,,,... 
If immediately goes your way, 
without scaling in, take profit

When Only Few Setups: Not Easy to See Them in Time 
EURUSD 5 min 
Looks easy and profitable, but very hard to do well 
There was about one 10 pip scalp each hour 
Difficult to watch carefully, 
when nothing is happening for many bars 
Very easy to miss many trades 
Very easy to make mistakes, 
that erase prior good trades

Forex: About 5 Setups in Each Session 
EURUSD 5 min 
EURUSD 5 min chart has 60 - 80 bars per session, 
on the 5 min chart, that move enough to trade 
An average session has at least five 10 pip scalps 
An average session has at least one 20 pip scalp 
Some sessions have many more

Forex: About 5 Setups in Each Session 
EURUSD 5 min 
Some experienced traders scalp 
It takes years of experience, 
as their primary style of trading 
to scalp consistently profitably 
Many experts are drawn to scalping, 
because it offers the greatest profit potential 
It also has to suit your personality, 
because trading needs to be fun, 
if you are doing it forever

Increase Probability: Wide Stops and Scaling In 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Although average 5 minute bar, 
is less than the minimum 10 pip scalp, 
the range of every 2 - 5 bars is more than 10 pips 
Enough room for a scalp entry every 2 - 5 bars 
This is especially true if a trader: 
Can use a 30 pip stop (swing stop) 
Is comfortable using limit and market orders, 
as well as stop orders 
Is able to scale in 
Today had many bars that 
were at least 10 pips tall

Why Beginners Scalp: Smallest Risk per Trade 
EURUSD 5 min 
Remember my 90% rule: 
For 90% of bars on chart, 
can make money buying or selling, 
if managed correctly 
Scaling in increases risk, 
but that is more than offset, by the 
great increase in probability of a profitable trade, 
in the hands of a skilled scalper

Weak selloff 
Probably bear leg in TR, not bear trend 
Bulls buy 2nd reversal up, and new lows 
Exit breakeven on 1st buy, 
and 10 pips profit on 2nd buy

EURUSD 5 min 
Many setups do not make 10 pips 
Need fast, good management 
Manual high frequency trading 
Extremely difficult to do profitably 
Traders should not scalp for less than 10 pips 
Great traders can scalp for 10 - 20 pips all session, 
and win on 90% of trades 
Very few can ever do that long-term

Why Beginners Scalp: Smallest Risk per Trade 
EU RUSO 5 min 
Beginners are drawn to scalping, 
because each trade has smaller risk 
Many beginners worked so hard, to get a 
small account, that they are terrified of losing it 
The result is that all they see is risk, 
and ignore reward, and especially probability 
Traders need to think of all 3 variables in 
the Trader's Equation, all of the time 
Otherwise, the variables they ignore, 
will cost them a lot of money

Scalping: Most Traders Take Some Scalps 
EURUSD 5 min 
This video has many examples of scalps that 
most traders can occasionally spot in time to take, 
and then manage well enough to be profitable 
2 big bull trend bars 
Buy close of strong bull bar 
Exit at 1st buy 
Breakeven on 1st buy 
Scalp profit on 2nd buy 
If falls for size of scalp, buy more

Scalping: Easy to Miss Most Scalp Setups 
EURUSD 5 min 
If you spot a scalp in time, and 
think you can manage it well, then take it 
Most of the time, you will spot them too late 
Don't worry about taking many 
Most of your money will come 
from swing trades, not scalps

Strong Consecutive Bull Bars on Open: Always In Long 
EURUSD 5 mtn 
2nd Leg Up in TR, Leg 1 = Leg 2 
Nested Wedge 
Failed BO above TR 
Good sell signal bar 
Place stop order to sell 1 pip 
Below signal bar 
Can put protective stop 1 pip above 
Can put limit order 
-
to take profit 10 pips lower 
Small sell signal bar 
Some bears will put a stop 
more than Measured Move up 
and will scale in higher

10 pips below entry

Entering with Stop Order: Only If at Top or Bottom of TR 
EU RUSO 5 min 
When the legs, range, and bars are small, 
it is usually easier to make money entering with 
limit orders, using wide stops, and scaling in 
If there is a good bull bar or Micro DB 
near a possible low of the range, 
bulls will buy with a stop, 
especially if can use wide stop and scale in 
If there is a good bear signal bar or Micro OT 
near a possible top of the range, 
bears will sell with a stop, 
especially if can use wide stop and scale in

TR Denial Syndrome: TR, but You Still Buy High and Sell Low! 
EURUSD 5 min 
Beginners see what is likely a TR 
They know the best way to trade is to 
Buy Low, Sell High, Scalp (BLSHS) 
Yet, they continue to do the opposite 
They keep selling bear BOs near the bottom 
They sell at prior low and 
close of big bear bar near prior low 
They keep buying bull BOs near the top 
They buy at prior high and 
close of big bull bar near prior high

80°/o Rule: In TR, 80°/o of BO Attempts Fail and Reverse 
EURUSD 5 min 
Beginners know my 80% rule, but 
do not believe it is really going to work this time 
80% of BO attempts in a TR fail 
In a TR, it is much easier to make money 
selling a strong bull BO and buying a strong bear BO 
~tA 
l"1 
Beginners constantly look for TR to BO into trend 
Beginners always find a reason why this time will be different 
They lose on every trade

Scalping on Higher Time Frame: Same Approach 
EU RUSO 60 min 
Sometimes range on 60 min chart is 
only 20 pips tall for 40 or more bars 
When scalping, easier to make money 
if wait for bars to be at least 20 pips tall, 
and height of TR over the past 10 bars 
to be at least 50 pips tall

Scalping: Easier If Many Bars Bigger Than Minimum Scalp 
EURUSD 60 min 
Easier to make money if profit target 
is smaller than size of average bar 
Need at least 10 pips profit, 
else you will lose 
Reasonable stop is about 
3 times bigger (30 pips) than reward 
Allows room to scale in, if needed 
Scaling in adds risk, but increases probability 
If bars stay at only 10 - 20 pips tall, 
which is small for a 60 min chart, 
then ok to scalp for only 10 - 20 pips 
Since each bar is 60 min, 
do not get many trades per session

Wide Stop: Allows Scaling In, Increases Probability (and Risk!) 
EURUSD 60 min 
4 bear bars, 3 are big 
Parabolic Sell Climax 
Strong enough selling so 
2nd Leg Down likely 
Can take 10 pips profit 
Bars up to this point 
are big enough for 20 pip scalp 
Sell at high of prior bar 
Bet on failed reversal up 
Need wide stop to allow scaling in higher 
Might get 50% PB at any time 
Do not want to allow wide stop to get hit

EURUSD 60 min 
Bulls can take 10 pips profit 
if Actual Risk less than 10 pips 
Bulls can take 20 pips profit, 
which is near top of Sell Climax and 
also a reasonable profit target 
Bulls can take profit at MA 
or on reversal down

Beginners lose most of their money 
by mistakes due to incorrect beliefs (Psychology) 
This video is on losing trades that beginners 
make by mistake, and how to avoid or fix them 
~+~9•++++□1Qo□•+•1□•• 
• ++o 
~ 
I discuss how to handle good trades that go bad 
(Market Risk, which means bad luck) in the videos 
on Losing When Good Trade Goes Bad 
Shorter video set because traders 
lose more money from mistakes! 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com

@Albrooksall

Beginners: Lose Because of Ignorance 
Ignorance does not mean low IQ 
Means lack of information 
Smart person can be ignorant of some 
ancient culture because he never studied it 
Ignorance is barrier to making money 
Easy to fix by simply spending time 
being open to learning 
or thought about it

2. Noise exists 
3. Most of time, either only bulls or bears can make money

Beginners: Bad Management Decisions 
Beginner sells, 
betting on 
Beginner buys 
Loses, despite good trade 
Does not want much risk so 
puts stop below signal bar 
Beginners are often wrong in picking entries, 
usually because they focus too much on risk 
and not enough on probability or reward 
Beginners often make bad management decisions 
Even when they know what the right thing to do is 
They conclude that this trade is an exception 
that requires them to do something else

but manages as scalp 
Not enough risk when 50 - 60% probability 
of swing profit

Decides never really willing to risk to swing stop 
Tightens stop to scalper's stop

90°/o of Bars: Can Buy or Sell and Make Money 
90% of the bars are in channel or TR 
40 - 60% chance direction will continue or reverse 
Probability for bulls and bears is between 40 - 60% 
during 90% of the bars on every chart 
-
Bad management is the major cause of losses 
Must manage position size, risk, and reward 
If fail at any step, you will lose money 
From Trader's Equation, trader knows that 
even with only 40% probability, 
it is winning strategy to exit with reward 
at least 2 times Actual Risk

Bad Management: Causes Most Losses 
From the Trader's Equation, trader knows that 
even with only 40% probability, 
it is winning strategy to exit with reward 
at least 2 times Actual Risk 
c!>~ 
o• 
~~+9 
+'++□1Qo +,i□••• 
~• 9++0 
~ 
This means that during 90% of the bars, 
both bulls and bears can make money 
by managing trades correctly 
The reason for most losses is bad management 
When managed well, 90% of time can get out 
breakeven (without a loss)

bear reversal bar

from Micro DB test of 1st entry

Minor reversal means low reward

Buy signal bar was reversal bar

8 bars without bull body so no Buying Pressure 
Tight Bear Channel so minor reversal at best 
Bear follow-through bar after big bear BO 
so Sell The Close bar

that he wants very high probability

he thinks it is confirmation that trend is strong

might be a climactic end of the trend, 
the bar just looks too strong

him at least a little profit

• Rules to avoid big losses

Avoid Big Loss: Always Follow These Rules 
40 - 60% of average reasonable trades 
will lose at least a little money 
Exit if reasonable signal in opposite direction 
If reversal fails, can always put position back on 
(great scalpers win 90% of time, but difficult to do) Exit immediately if clearly wrong, like if 
there is reversal with consecutive strong bars 
Sell DT Bear Flag

Slide 2 
@Albrooksall

Most Important Protection: Protective Stop in the Market 
The most important protection from 
a loss growing too large is a protective stop 
A stop protects you from yourself 
more than from the market 
Always have stop in the market, not in your head 
If you only have it in your head, 
you will quickly discover that it too often 
stays in your head, and never reaches the market 
The result is a huge loss that wipes out 
the last 20 things you did well 
10•10tt,

Slide 3 
@Albrooksall

Beginner buys High 1 Bull Flag 
(PB in strong bull trend) 
Why does he not exit after big bear BO 
and bear follow-through bar? 
We were genetically bred to be hopeful 
People long ago who were in trouble, 
but gave up, died, and so did their genes 
People long ago who remained hopeful, 
and never gave up, had a much better chance 
of passing their genes on to us 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 4 
@Albrooksall

Hard to Admit Wrong: Nobody Likes a Quitter 
Exiting with a loss is giving up 
Beginner who is in a bad trade, 
often refuses to give up 
Often makes it worse by adding to bad trade 
Social stigma against quitting 
Bad to not keep trying 
Only lazy cowards give up

a live coward, than a dead hero

Never Lose Sight of Goal: Make Money 
Beginner works hard to save enough money to 
open a small account and start trading 
That money is symbolic of 
birth of new life as trader 
Trader's Equation has 3 variables: 
Risk (distance to stop) 
Reward (distance to profit target) 
Probability (the odds of making money 
before the stop is hit) 
All 3 have to work together to create profit 
Avoiding risk is not enough

Beginner fears an early death as a trader,

low probability trades

you will lose money

Main Points: Scaling In Creates Big Losses

■ Scaling In creates big losses

Slide 8 
@Albrooksall

Biggest Losses: Scaling In, but Position Size Too Big 
Traders scale in to increase probability 
Since now bigger position, more dollars at risk 
Single costliest mistake beginner makes is 
not reducing position size 
when scaling in and stop is far 
Beginners know that scaling in 
increases probability of profit 
Only experienced traders should scale in 
When scaling in is combined with 
being too hopeful instead of objective, 
can get big loss quickly, 
and give back profit from last 10 wins 
They plan to scale in, 
but quickly realize their size is too big 
Their plan was unrealistic 
Must trade very small if scaling in 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Slide 9 
@Albrooksall

Biggest Losses: Only Looking at 2 Bars, Not Entire Chart 
Strong reversal up from HL MTR 
Accelerating up, 2 big bull bars 
Some bears see strong bulls 
so will wait for 2nd signal or 
strong bear reversal to sell 
Buy The Close bull trend 
Probability is bulls will buy 1st PB 
because some bulls trapped out 
and looking for PB to buy 
Location of reasonable stop choices 
is usually obvious 
Stop 
BrooksTradingCourse.com

Slide 10 
@Albrooksall

Biggest Losses: If Stop Is Far, Trade Small 
If willing to scale in 2 more times 
for total of 3 entries, 
want each entry to be 
about 1/3rd of normal position size 
Must trade small I 
If stop is twice as far as normal, 
then each new position is 1/6 normal size 
If cannot trade that small, then do not take 3 entries 
Instead, take one and wait for PB 
Wait to buy above bull reversal bar 
Stop

Stop

and strong emotion 
Easy to ignore rules 
about trading small

reality that still in TR

Biggest Losses: Ignoring opposite Arguments 
Even when the context and momentum are good, 
there is always a valid reason to take the 
opposite side of every trade, 
especially when still in a TR 
If strong leg, 
but not BO far beyond TR, 
look for possible 2nd Leg Trap 
Not obvious unless you think possible 
One of most common mistakes is believing that 
another leg is likely after a strong leg 
It is only likely if the BO is clearly above TR, 
especially if already a 2nd Leg Up within the TR

Rules: 3 Types of Protection for Every Trade 
For every trade, use these 3 types of protection: 
Always have a reasonable protective stop in 
the market, and trade small if it is far away 
Exit if reasonable opposite signal 
( does not have to be perfect or strong) 
Exit if there is a strong, opposite BO

Strong Trends: Eventual Disappointment 
Buy The Close bull trends and 
Sell The Close bear trends are common 
Each eventually has a disappointing bar or two 
that can lead to at least a minor reversal, 
and a possible TR or major reversal 
Most traders can avoid a loss 
if they manage their trades well 
I discuss management in the videos on 
"Entering Late in Trends" and 
will briefly discuss it again here

Buy The Close: Always Has Final Trend Bar 
Once bull believes it is a Buy The Close bull trend, 
he buys one or more closes 
If the bar following his buy is a strong bear bar, 
he suspects that he just bought the 
final bull trend bar

Easiest Acceptable Management: Just Rely on Stop 
Easiest approach is to rely on stop 
As long as premise is still valid and stop not hit, 
trader holds onto his position 
For example, bull buys the close, 
but the next bar is bear bar and the next 2 - 3 bars 
did not allow him to get out breakeven 
If disappointed by the strength of the selloff, 
just get out breakeven on a test of the entry price 
(the highest close) 
Common cause of OT (or DB)

Scale In: Increments Based on Size of Smallest Scalp 
Some bulls will scale in 1- 3 times, 
with each one based on size of the smallest scalp 
Some scale in below low of prior bar, 
as long as each entry is at least the 
size of a scalp below the last buy 
Trade small enough so that if stop is hit, 
loss is no more than with any other trade

Disappointment: Exit to Avoid Loss, Not to Make Profit 
If disappointed by the strength of the selloff, 
just get out breakeven on 1st buy and 
with a profit on the lower buys 
Scale in to increase probability of profit, 
or chance of avoiding loss 
Only for experts because have to manage well 
One mistake can quickly undo 10 good trades

Slide 16 
@Albrooksall

Disappointed, but No PB: Get out Breakeven 
Some bulls will scale in only one time 
They wait for a PB that is at least 2 - 3 times 
bigger than a minimum scalp 
They then buy above the high of a bull reversal bar 
They only buy if the distance from the 1st entry 
is at least 2 - 3 times the size of a minimum scalp 
(not here) 
If the PB does not go deep enough, 
they wi11 just hold their original position 
If disappointed by the selling, 
they will exit breakeven

Disappointing Follow-Through: Scale In to Exit without Loss 
Quick thinking bears scaled in higher 
at the size of a scalp above 
Believed bear BO was strong enough 
So that 1st reversal up would fail 
Stop

Breakeven on 1st sell and 
scalper's profit on 2nd

Disappointing Follow-Through: Bears Try to Exit Breakeven 
4 consecutive strong bear bars 
New low 
Sell The Close bear trend 
Bear sells closes 
Bears scale in, at least the size of a scalp higher 
Disappointed that scale in bar 
closed on its high, 
creating a big bull bar 
-- Stop 
Bears place limit order to buy at the low close 
Now want to get out breakeven on 1st entry 
and with scalp profit on 2nd entry

Bears Cannot Get out Breakeven: Will Buy Higher 
Fell to limit order, but not below 
Most bears unable to buy back shorts 
Getting worried about possible loss 
Raised limit order slightly, even though will 
then have only small net profit 
Hit new limit order, 
but again not filled 
Bears are trapped 
Bears start buying back at market 
and above 2 bull buy signal bars 
-- Stop

Reasonable Entries: Usually Get Tested within 1 - 3 Bars 
When there is a reasonable entry, 
and then an immediate reversal 
for 1 or more bars, 
the entry usually gets tested 
If not within 1- 3 bars, 
then within 20 bars 
"Test" means within 
1- 2 ticks or pips 
Disappointed trader usually 
can exit breakeven 
or with small loss 
Sell The Close bear trend 
Final entry was followed by big bull bar

Breakeven on 1st attempt, 
they raised limit order 
Still could not get out, gave up, 
and resulted in rally 
When bears give up, odds favor 2 legs up 
Bears desperate for PB to buy back shorts 
with smaller loss 
Bulls want PB to buy, since expect 
bears will not sell for many more bars ¢

Do Not Let Losses Grow: Can Always Buy Again Later 
If bought the high close and reverses down strongly, 
you missed a topping pattern and a sell signal 
Even if you still do not see it, 
get out because the market is telling you 
that you are wrong 
Do not get paralyzed by a need to see 
what you missed 
Just exit with a loss before it grows big 
o9•+, 
l 
~! 
When bulls give up, 
odds favor a swing down and probably 
Ten Bars, Two Legs (TBTL) 
Can always buy again if 
immediately reverses back up
